## INV-118: each lane runs a `setsid`-detached guardian holding the read end of `guard.fifo`; the wrapper's write end is opened before the guardian ever spawns, so kernel EOF on any death ‚Äî including SIGKILL/OOM ‚Äî triggers an idempotent, lane-scoped reap

_Triage (issue #236): [machine-checked: tests/unit/test-lane-gc-p5-guardian.sh]_

**Rule** (Lane-GC series PR-5, design `docs/designs/lane-containment-gc.md` ¬ß4-C3, drafted as INV-109 in the design doc ‚Äî renumbered here per the numbering note below): immediately after a successful `lane_install` (and before any other background work ‚Äî auth setup, the token daemon, the heartbeat), both wrappers install a per-lane death watch:

1. **FIFO open-order contract (load-bearing).** `mkfifo "${ADT_LANE_DIR}/guard.fifo"` ‚Üí `exec {ADT_GUARD_FD}<>"${ADT_LANE_DIR}/guard.fifo"` (the wrapper's WRITE end, opened `<>` so it never blocks) ‚Üí **only then** `setsid bash lib-guardian.sh --lane-dir "$ADT_LANE_DIR" &`. This ordering guarantees the guardian's own read-side `exec 3<"$FIFO_PATH"` always finds a writer already present and returns immediately ‚Äî never blocking on `open(2)`. `setsid` is a hard prerequisite (a same-PGID guardian would die with the wrapper on every group-form kill, the routine re-dispatch path, silently degrading the fast reaper to GC-only); a host missing `setsid` logs a loud, actionable error (mentions `brew install util-linux` for macOS) and **degrades to no guardian this run** rather than aborting the wrapper ‚Äî bricking every dispatch on a `setsid`-less host would be worse than falling back to the periodic GC ([INV-117]) as the sole backstop.
2. **FD hygiene at every background spawn site.** `{ADT_GUARD_FD}` is not close-on-exec by default (verified empirically: it survives `exec()` into any binary, and a plain forked/backgrounded subshell inherits it too) ‚Äî so every long-lived spawn site this series already has (the agent CLI spawn in `_run_with_timeout`, the heartbeat loop, both token daemons, `lane_kill`'s and the sigterm-trap's escalators, `_bounded_call`'s background invocation, the review side's smoke-probe and fan-out subshells) closes its own inherited copy immediately: `[[ -n "${ADT_GUARD_FD:-}" ]] && exec {ADT_GUARD_FD}>&-`. The guardian's OWN spawn site closes it too, for the identical reason ‚Äî without that close the guardian becomes a second write-mode holder of the very fifo it watches, and EOF then never arrives even after the wrapper's real writer closes (found and fixed empirically while building this PR's fixtures ‚Äî a real bug, not merely a theoretical one). The design's own ¬ß10 residual wording is reused verbatim here: this is a literal-spawn-site grep-pin ‚Äî pipeline subshells, `bash -c '‚Ä¶'` strings embedded in other strings, or dynamically constructed commands are not (and cannot be) caught; a missed site degrades EOF from "wrapper died" to "subtree died", never a false kill.
3. **The guardian body** (`lib-guardian.sh`, deliberately `lib-*`-named so `install-project-hooks.sh` never symlinks it ‚Äî reached purely via each wrapper's own `readlink -f`-resolved `LIB_DIR`, same contract as every sibling `lib-*.sh`): arms a 15s no-writer watchdog (`ADT_GUARDIAN_NO_WRITER_GRACE_SECONDS`, test-overridable) via a backgrounded timer + `SIGUSR2` trap **BEFORE** attempting `exec 3<"$FIFO_PATH"` ‚Äî never after (see the ordering-bug note below) ‚Äî then opens the fifo, disarms the watchdog once open returns, arms a chunked-PPID-style hard lifetime cap (`AGENT_TIMEOUT_SECONDS + 3600`, 60s-chunked so a SIGKILLed guardian's own chunk-watchdog dies within one chunk rather than surviving to the full cap ‚Äî the same anti-monolithic-sleep pattern [INV-79] mandates for the token daemon), then blocks on `read -r _ <&3`. EOF (wrapper died by ANY means), the no-writer watchdog, or the lifetime cap all converge on the same `do_reap`.
4. **`do_reap` is idempotent and lane-scoped.** It takes `reap.lock` itself via a single **non-blocking** `flock -n` (never a blocking `-w`) and, on success, calls `_kill_group_escalate` **directly** over the recorded `pgids` ‚Äî never through `lane_kill`, which would try to re-flock the SAME lock file `do_reap` already holds and self-deadlock for `lane_kill`'s own 10s bound (verified empirically: `flock` on a second fd against an already-self-held lock file blocks/deadlocks, it is not a re-entrant no-op). It skips (zero kills) when `STATE ‚àà {clean-exit, cleaning, gc-reaped, reaped-by-guardian}` ‚Äî the graceful-handshake and GC-already-reaped cases; tolerates ENOENT on the whole lane dir (GC rule 1.4 may have `rm -rf`'d it after killing a wedged guardian first) as "someone else finished"; escalates every distinct recorded pgid concurrently (one `setsid`-isolated escalator per pgid, so wall-clock stays ~grace regardless of count); then runs a **lane-scoped escape sweep** ‚Äî same-uid `/proc` enumeration matching `ADT_LANE_ID=<this lane's id>` exactly (a foreign, even registered, lane's tag is skipped, never swept) and unconditionally skipping any candidate whose env also carries `TERM_PROGRAM` (operator fail-safe) ‚Äî TERM ‚Üí 2s ‚Üí KILL per hit; finally promotes `STATE=reaped-by-guardian` and removes the fifo.
5. **Graceful path.** `cleanup()` in both wrappers, immediately after its `lane_reap` reap-first block and the PID-file/heartbeat teardown but **before** any network work (the FIFO handshake slot [INV-115] reserved for this PR), sends `{ printf 'done\n' >&"$ADT_GUARD_FD"; } 2>/dev/null || true; exec {ADT_GUARD_FD}>&- 2>/dev/null || true`. The guardian wakes on the resulting EOF, sees a terminal/in-progress `STATE`, and exits with zero kills ‚Äî the printed payload is cosmetic (the guardian treats any line, or bare EOF, identically as a wake); it exists only to give `guardian.log` a distinguishable "graceful" data point.

**Ordering-bug note (found and fixed while writing this PR's own test suite, not merely documented as a design residual):** the design's own ¬ß4-C3 pseudocode arms the no-writer watchdog check AFTER the blocking fifo open. If no writer is EVER present, that `open(2)` call blocks indefinitely, and a check placed after it is dead code for the exact scenario it claims to guard ‚Äî control never reaches it. This PR arms the timer + `SIGUSR2` trap FIRST, so the timer's `kill -USR2 $$` can interrupt the blocked `open(2)` syscall directly (verified empirically: a trap fires and exits while a sibling shell is parked in `exec 3<fifo` against a fifo nobody ever opened for write, exactly like it interrupts a blocked `read`). This also drops the design's proposed `/proc`-wide "scan every process for an existing writer" mechanism entirely ‚Äî unnecessary (a plain read-only fifo open already returns immediately if ANY writer is connected, by POSIX fifo semantics, with no separate check needed) and, empirically, too slow on a busy multi-tenant host to trust inside a 15s grace window. The timer-first approach needs no OS-specific introspection at all ‚Äî only signals and a plain fifo open ‚Äî so it is simultaneously simpler, faster, and portable to macOS for free (where the original `/proc`-based mechanism would not have worked at all).

**Numbering note**: the design doc (`docs/designs/lane-containment-gc.md` ¬ß8) drafted this as INV-109 at design time. By this PR's rebase onto `origin/main`, the head had advanced to [INV-117] (Lane-GC PR-4's periodic GC, #380/#427) ‚Äî so this PR claims the next free slot, **INV-118**, per the same repo-wide "first-merged keeps it, renumber on collision" convention the three prior PRs in this series already applied.

**Producer**: `skills/autonomous-dispatcher/scripts/lib-guardian.sh` (the guardian body, `do_reap`, both watchdogs); `autonomous-dev.sh`/`autonomous-review.sh` (the install-order block right after `lane_install`, and the `cleanup()` handshake); `lib-agent.sh`/`lib-auth.sh`/`lib-lane.sh` (the FD-hygiene close at every long-lived spawn site this series already ships).

**Consumer**: none outside this lane's own lifecycle ‚Äî a future GC pass ([INV-117]) reads the `STATE=reaped-by-guardian` transition this PR produces exactly as it already reads `STATE=clean-exit`/`gc-reaped`; [INV-117]'s own rule 1.2 (skip a dead lane whose guardian is alive and `STATE=reaping` for under 5 minutes) was already written in anticipation of this PR landing.

**Status**: **ENFORCED**, Lane-GC PR-5, closes #381.

**Test**: `tests/unit/test-lane-gc-p5-guardian.sh` ‚Äî the no-writer watchdog fires close to its configured grace even when nothing is EVER written (the ordering-bug regression, run under an outer `timeout` backstop) and a structural grep-pin confirms the trap precedes the fifo-open line; a SIGKILL-simulated wrapper session (closing its write fd) is reaped ‚Äî the recorded pgid dies and `STATE` promotes to `reaped-by-guardian` ‚Äî within grace(10s)+2s; the lane-scoped escape sweep kills a same-lane-tagged escapee but never a differently-(registered-but-foreign-)lane-tagged one; FD-hygiene's sole-holder-vs-inherited-fd EOF-deferral behavior (both branches asserted); the graceful handshake produces a zero-kill exit with `STATE` left exactly where the wrapper's own `cleanup()` set it; the lifetime cap fires under an accelerated test-only override and its chunk-watchdog dies within one chunk of a SIGKILLed guardian; `do_reap`'s EOF-triggered reap racing a concurrent `lane_kill` call serializes on `reap.lock` to exactly one `SIGKILL` (no double-kill) and a grep-pin proves `do_reap`'s CODE (not its doc comments, which reference the name in prose) never calls `lane_kill` itself; ENOENT tolerance against a lane dir removed before the guardian ever wakes; the wrapper install-order grep-pins (`mkfifo` before the write-fd open, the write-fd open before the guardian spawn, the guardian's own spawn line closing its inherited fd); the `setsid`-absent branch degrades (no `exit`) with an actionable `util-linux` mention; FD-hygiene close-guard counts at every touched file. `tests/e2e/run-lane-gc-p5-guardian-e2e.sh` (TC-LGC5-E2E-01) drives the REAL, unmodified `autonomous-dev.sh` (fixture `gh` + fixture `claude` on `PATH`) far enough to install the lane and guardian, SIGKILLs the entire wrapper session, and asserts the fixture agent is gone within grace+2s and `STATE=reaped-by-guardian` ‚Äî the issue's stated E2E acceptance criterion, surfaced as its own CI job independent of the unit suite.

**Cross-references**:
- [INV-109] (lane registry) ‚Äî the `reap.lock`/`pgids`/`STATE` fields the guardian reads and writes are this registry's own files; the guardian invents no parallel state.
- [INV-110] (universal lane tagging) ‚Äî the `ADT_LANE_ID` tag the escape sweep matches on.
- [INV-114] (kill-escalation contract) ‚Äî the guardian's pgid escalation reuses the exact same `_kill_group_escalate` primitive every other kill path in this series uses.
- [INV-115] (bounded, ordered teardown) ‚Äî the FIFO handshake this PR ships lands in the exact slot that invariant reserved ("feature-guarded as a no-op ‚Ä¶ PR-5 is a pure addition, not a re-ordering").
- [INV-117] (periodic GC) ‚Äî the non-load-bearing backstop for a guardian that itself dies (OOM, host reboot, a bug); GC's rule 1.2/1.3/1.4 already anticipate this PR's `STATE=reaping`/`reaped-by-guardian` values.
- `docs/designs/lane-containment-gc.md` ¬ß4-C3, ¬ß9 PR-5, ¬ß10 (fd-hygiene residual) ‚Äî the full guardian design this invariant implements; `docs/designs/lane-gc-p5-guardian.md` ‚Äî this PR's numbering re-verification, the ordering-bug fix, and the setsid-degradation decision.

---

## INV-119: `dispatch-local.sh` refuses to spawn under box distress (exit 75, EX_TEMPFAIL) after one bounded reclaim attempt, and a remote-backend deferral surfaces as a fourth `DEFERRED` liveness verdict ‚Äî never a crash

_Triage (issue #236): [machine-checked: tests/unit/test-lane-gc-p6-gate.sh]_

**Rule** (Lane-GC series PR-6, design `docs/designs/lane-containment-gc.md` ¬ß4-C6, drafted as INV-112 in the design doc ‚Äî renumbered here per the numbering note below): before `kill_stale_wrapper`/spawn, `dispatch-local.sh` evaluates four admission signals against the box's real (or test-overridden) state ‚Äî three (load/core, MemAvailable floor, global live-lane count) strictly INDEPENDENT; the swap signal is conditionally gated by available-memory headroom, see the **Amendment note (2026-07-08, #441)** below:

1. **`load1 / nproc > GATE_LOAD_PER_CORE`** (default 3) ‚Äî via `lib-lane.sh::box_health`'s `load1_per_core` field (Linux `/proc/loadavg`+`nproc`; macOS/BSD `sysctl -n vm.loadavg`/`hw.ncpu`).
2. **`MemAvailable < GATE_MIN_MEM_MB`** (default 2048 MB) ‚Äî `box_health`'s `mem_available_mb` (Linux `/proc/meminfo`; macOS/BSD `vm_stat`'s free+inactive pages √ó page size).
3. **`swap-used% > GATE_SWAP_PCT`** (default 90) ‚Äî `box_health`'s `swap_pct`, **floor-rounded** (never round-to-nearest ‚Äî a steady 90.4x% must never flap across the default-90 boundary tick to tick; `printf %.0f` rounds-to-nearest and was replaced by an integer-floor computation for this reason, review P3-1) (Linux `/proc/meminfo` Swap{Total,Free}; macOS/BSD `sysctl vm.swapusage`). **[#441 amendment]** this signal alone crossing its ceiling no longer fires the gate ‚Äî it must ALSO be the case that `mem_available_mb < GATE_MIN_MEM_MB * GATE_SWAP_REQUIRES_MEM_MULTIPLE` (default multiple 3, so the rescue floor is 6144 MB with defaults). See the amendment note below.
4. **global live-lane count `‚â• MAX_TOTAL_CONCURRENT`** (default 12, cross-project ‚Äî distinct from the existing per-project `MAX_CONCURRENT`) ‚Äî `lib-lane.sh::lane_global_live_count`, registry-driven (joins on `lane_probe`'s own `live` verdict across every project's `autonomous-*/lanes/`, **short-circuiting once the running count reaches the caller's own cap** ‚Äî the gate only ever needs to know "at or above the cap," never the exact total, and a terminal-`STATE` lane is skipped without a `lane_probe` call at all, since a terminal state can never be live ‚Äî review P2-3), falling back to a live-PID-file count when NO `lanes/` directory exists anywhere under `ADT_STATE_ROOT` (a fresh host with no registry yet).

Any signal absent from `box_health`'s output (its underlying source unreadable) is treated as **unknown, never gating** ‚Äî the same fail-toward-leak posture this design series applies to kill decisions, applied here to admission: a min-floor check (MemAvailable) must never coerce an absent value to `0` (reads as "critically low"), and a max-ceiling check (load/swap) must never coerce an absent value to `0` either (reads as "perfectly healthy" ‚Äî masking that the signal is actually unknown).

**Refusal path**: log `dispatch deferred: back-pressure (<reason>)` ‚Üí one bounded `adt-gc.sh --quick` reclaim attempt (sharing entry-point resolution and timeout feature-detection with the pre-existing unconditional opportunistic call via `ADT_GC_ENTRY`/`_run_adt_gc_quick`) ‚Üí re-check all four signals ONCE ‚Üí if a signal still fires: touch a defer marker `$ADT_STATE_ROOT/autonomous-<PROJECT_ID>/lanes/.defer-<kind>-<issue_num>` (content = the fired reason; `kind` ‚àà `issue|review`, matching the existing PID-file kind vocabulary ‚Äî NOT the caller's own `dev-new|dev-resume|review` type token) ‚Üí **[#444, A2] source-level label revert** (see below) ‚Üí `exit 75` (EX_TEMPFAIL). If the re-check clears, dispatch proceeds normally. **The GATE ITSELF never kills, signals, or otherwise touches any running process** ‚Äî it is pure admission control (grep-pinned: no `kill`/`pkill`/`SIGTERM`/`SIGKILL` token anywhere in the gate's own code block). **Honest contract scope (review P2-1)**: the reclaim step the gate calls into, `adt-gc.sh --quick`, is a SEPARATE component under [INV-117]'s own safety predicate ‚Äî under that GC's default `--dry-run` mode the reclaim attempt classifies-only and kills nothing (the common case, and the case every existing gate test exercises); if an operator has separately opted a host into `ADT_GC_ENFORCE=1` (GC's own enforce-mode, [INV-117]), that SAME `--quick` call can perform a REAL kill of REGISTRY-DEAD-lane residue ‚Äî but that kill is authorized by INV-117's own decision table, not by this gate, and would fire identically whether or not the gate ever called it (the box-wide cron/opportunistic `--quick` calls already run regardless of gate state). The gate's "never kills" claim is therefore scoped precisely to the ADMISSION DECISION itself, never to the reclaim step's own (separately-authorized, separately-invariant-governed) side effects.

**[#444, A2] Source-level label revert ‚Äî the gate reverts its own dispatch's label, unconditionally.** Prior to this amendment, the ONLY revert path was `handle_dispatch_deferred` (below), which requires the DISPATCHING PROCESS to synchronously observe rc=75 ‚Äî a requirement the local backend can miss (the dispatching session ends before observing the exit code) and the remote-aws-ssm backend can NEVER meet (`dispatch-remote-aws-ssm.sh` returns 0 at SSM-accept, so the caller never sees rc=75 at all). Either gap leaves the issue's label stranded in an active state (`in-progress`/`reviewing`) with no self-healing path if no future tick ever runs for that project (observed live: #439/#440, stranded 10+ hours). The fix moves the revert to the one process GUARANTEED to run for every defer ‚Äî the gate itself, in `dispatch-local.sh`'s `_admission_gate`, immediately before `exit 75`: it sources ONLY `lib-issue-provider.sh` (never `lib-dispatch.sh` ‚Äî that 3700+-line lib's `:?`-guarded expansions can abort under this script's `set -euo pipefail` with a minimal conf) and calls `itp_transition_state` directly (`label_swap` is a one-line delegator to the same verb). Mapping: `dev-new`/`dev-resume` ‚Üí `in-progress ‚Üí pending-dev`; `review` ‚Üí `reviewing ‚Üí pending-review` (dev-new deliberately targets `pending-dev`, not bare `autonomous` ‚Äî verified chain: Step 4 dispatches dev-resume with an empty `SESSION_ID`, which `autonomous-dev.sh` normalizes to `MODE=new`, and `list_new_issues` excludes `pending-dev` so there is no double-dispatch window). **Fail-open, unconditionally**: every source and the `itp_transition_state` call are `2>/dev/null || true`-guarded ‚Äî a failed revert (unreachable code host, auth failure) changes nothing about the exit code or the defer marker, only logs one WARN naming the failure, and falls back to the pre-existing tick-side recovery below. Auth is one-shot (mints a single GitHub App token via `gh-app-token.sh` in `GH_AUTH_MODE=app`, matching `dispatcher-tick.sh`'s own one-shot pattern) ‚Äî deliberately NEVER `lib-auth.sh::setup_github_auth`, which spawns a background token-refresh daemon that would leak one per defer. Idempotent with `handle_dispatch_deferred`'s own revert (local backend, live dispatcher: both run) ‚Äî `gh issue edit`'s remove/add of an already-absent/-present label is a no-op at the API level.

**rc=75 attribution** (`lib-dispatch.sh`): `is_dispatch_deferred_rc` is the sentinel predicate (`[ "$rc" -eq 75 ]`); `handle_dispatch_deferred <issue> <mode> <revert_from_label> <revert_to_label>` is the shared recovery action, wired into every `dispatch()` call site (`dispatcher-tick.sh` Steps 2/3/4, and the two `dispatch dev-new` sites inside `lib-dispatch.sh` itself). It (a) releases the dispatch marker for (issue, mode) so the NEXT tick's `acquire_dispatch_marker` doesn't see a stale "concurrent tick owns this" marker sitting out its TTL for an attempt that never launched anything, and (b) **reverts the caller's own immediately-preceding `label_swap`** ‚Äî every `dispatch()` call site performs its own label swap (e.g. `"" ‚Üí in-progress`) BEFORE calling `dispatch()`, so under the LOCAL backend a defer that only stopped at "no further label change" would leave the issue stranded in an active state with NO wrapper ever spawned, no PID file, no near-success signal of any kind ‚Äî the very next tick's Step 5 would then misclassify that stranding as a genuine crash. Reverting the label makes a local-backend defer indistinguishable, from the issue's own label history, from "this tick never touched it": the issue naturally reappears in the SAME selector query next tick. Deliberately does NOT post a comment or call `count_retries`/`mark_stalled` ‚Äî a defer is invisible to the retry-budget economy by design. **A dispatch() call that fails for a REASON OTHER than the gate (any non-zero, non-75 rc)** is NOT treated as a defer: the pre-existing pre-P6 failure handling for that call site applies unchanged (release the marker; for the Step 2/3/4 tick sites this is the pre-P6 posture of leaving the label as `label_swap` already set it ‚Äî Step 5's own stale-detection is the pre-existing backstop for that case, exactly as it was before this PR; the two `lib-dispatch.sh` router sites explicitly branch `is_dispatch_deferred_rc` first and an `elif [ rc -ne 0 ]` second, so the two paths can never be conflated ‚Äî review P1-2).

**Remote-backend DEFERRED plumbing (three files ‚Äî under-scoping this to one file was a confirmed design-review finding)**: under `EXECUTION_BACKEND=remote-aws-ssm`, `dispatch()`'s underlying `dispatch-remote-aws-ssm.sh` returns 0 the instant SSM ACCEPTS the command ‚Äî the remote wrapper-host's own gate verdict is never observed synchronously by the local-backend catch above, so an unmarked deferral would masquerade as a no-PR crash on a LATER tick's Step 5b. Three files close this:

1. **`liveness-check-remote-aws-ssm.sh`**: the remote snippet checks a `.defer-<kind>-<issue>` marker under the wrapper host's own lane registry FIRST ‚Äî before the ALIVE/DEAD tiers. The marker is authorized as DEFERRED only when BOTH: (a) it is **not superseded** ‚Äî at or after the mtime of a `.attempt-<kind>-<issue>` token `dispatch-local.sh` writes UNCONDITIONALLY at the very start of every single invocation, before its own gate even runs (review P1-1: the controller-side `dispatch-marker-<issue>-<mode>` file [INV-108]'s `acquire_dispatch_marker` primitive is NOT usable as this anchor ‚Äî it lives on the DISPATCHER host, and under `remote-aws-ssm` the dispatcher and wrapper hosts are different machines; this snippet runs entirely on the wrapper host with no dispatcher-host filesystem or GitHub API access, so `dispatch-local.sh` ‚Äî which DOES run on the wrapper host for every attempt, local or SSM-invoked ‚Äî is the only component that can honestly record "the last dispatch attempt on THIS host" the snippet can read); and (b) it is still within `DEFER_MARKER_MAX_AGE_SECONDS` (default 900s) of "now". Condition (a) alone is not the full mechanism: since nothing re-dispatches an already-active issue to refresh the attempt token, a defer that only checked (a) would persist indefinitely once the box has been under pressure even once, never expiring into the existing crash-declare ‚Üí pending-dev ‚Üí retry recovery path ‚Äî condition (b) is what eventually un-sticks that case, so both bounds apply together, not one as a mere fallback for the other. When the attempt marker is missing/unstattable (e.g. a pre-upgrade wrapper host that has never run the updated `dispatch-local.sh`), condition (a) degrades to vacuously true and (b) alone gates ‚Äî the exact pre-fix bare-age-window behavior, now scoped to that one case. An out-of-date (superseded) or too-old defer marker is ignored entirely, falling through unconditionally to the normal ALIVE/DEAD tiers below ‚Äî a stale defer must never shadow a real DEAD verdict. Emits `DEFERRED\n<age_s>` (two lines, and **exactly** two lines ‚Äî nothing after; a third line or trailing garbage after the age falls through to indeterminate, never a fabricated DEFERRED, mirroring the same anchoring rigor the pre-existing ALIVE/DEAD tiers already apply, review P2-2) as a fourth verdict alongside the existing `ALIVE`/`DEAD`/empty tri-state.
2. **`lib-dispatch.sh::_remote_pid_alive_query`**: parses the driver's two-line DEFERRED stdout into a single colon-joined token (`DEFERRED:<age_s>`) ‚Äî its own return value is consumed via `$(...)` command substitution, so a bare embedded newline would either collapse under word-splitting or defeat a caller's exact-match `case`.
3. **`lib-dispatch.sh::pid_alive`**: a side-channel (`PID_ALIVE_LAST_VERDICT`/`PID_ALIVE_LAST_DEFERRED_AGE`, reset to empty at the TOP of every call, not merely the remote-backend branch) carries the verdict past `pid_alive`'s own boolean return contract, which has no third code-path slot for a 4th verdict and which dozens of pre-existing callers already depend on staying exactly boolean. `pid_alive` itself still returns 1 (not-alive ‚Äî correct: no wrapper was launched) for DEFERRED. DEFERRED is checked and short-circuits BEFORE the `--at-cap` exception ([INV-30]) ‚Äî a back-pressure defer is a DEFINITE verdict from the wrapper host's own gate, never an "indeterminate transport" condition, and at-cap's indeterminate‚ÜíDEAD policy must never reinterpret it.
4. **`dispatcher-tick.sh` Step 5b**: fast-returns on `PID_ALIVE_LAST_VERDICT=DEFERRED` immediately upon entering the DEAD branch ‚Äî BEFORE the no-PR / near-success crash-declaration checks for both dev and review kinds. No comment, no label flip, no retry decrement; just a log line and continue to the next candidate. **[#444, B1 edit 2]** this fast-return now carries an age bound: when `PID_ALIVE_LAST_DEFERRED_AGE >= DEFER_MARKER_MAX_AGE_SECONDS` (the same existing knob, default 900), it falls through to `_revert_defer_strand` (label revert, no comment, no retry decrement) instead of the plain `continue`. Currently unreachable in production for ages at/past the threshold ‚Äî the remote probe (`liveness-check-remote-aws-ssm.sh`) itself stops reporting DEFERRED past its own `DEFER_MAX_AGE` ‚Äî so this is deliberate, documented belt-and-suspenders against a future probe change, matching the local-backend expiry rule below rather than leaving a theoretical unbounded active-label window. **[review P1, #444]** `_revert_defer_strand`'s own success/failure is checked here (never swallowed) ‚Äî a failed revert (code host still unreachable) still `continue`s without a crash comment or retry decrement (an expired defer never becomes a crash regardless of revert outcome), but is logged distinctly so the operator can see the label may still be stranded; there is no marker on this remote side-channel path to prematurely consume, unlike edit 1 below.

**[#444, B1 edit 1] Defer-marker lifecycle gains an expiry ‚Üí revert-not-crash rule on the LOCAL backend.** Pre-#444, the local backend never consulted the defer marker at all in Step 5 ‚Äî a post-defer tick that ran BEFORE `handle_dispatch_deferred` could observe rc=75 (the dispatching session ended first) misclassified the stranded active label as a genuine crash: false crash comment posted, retry budget burned, exactly the misattribution this whole invariant exists to prevent. `dispatcher-tick.sh` Step 5's DEAD branch now checks, under `EXECUTION_BACKEND=local` (dispatcher and wrapper host are the SAME machine, so the marker `dispatch-local.sh` wrote is directly file-readable ‚Äî no SSM round-trip needed), a local analog of the remote snippet's own freshness comparison (`lib-dispatch.sh::_local_defer_marker_verdict`, same marker path `_local_defer_marker_path` computes, same not-superseded-by-a-later-`.attempt-<kind>-<N>`-token check, same `DEFER_MARKER_MAX_AGE_SECONDS` threshold):
   - **FRESH** (marker exists, not superseded, age < threshold) ‚Üí fast-return: no comment, no label change, no retry decrement ‚Äî the local-backend analog of Step 5b's remote fast-return.
   - **EXPIRED** (not superseded, age ‚â• threshold) ‚Üí `_revert_defer_strand` reverts the label (`in-progress ‚Üí pending-dev` / `reviewing ‚Üí pending-review`), posts NO crash comment, decrements NO retry budget. **[review P1, #444]** the stale marker is removed ONLY after `_revert_defer_strand` reports success ‚Äî if the revert fails (the same unreachable-code-host condition that may have caused the original strand can still be in effect), the marker is deliberately KEPT so the NEXT tick sees the same EXPIRED verdict and retries the revert, rather than silently downgrading to `NONE` (no marker) on the very next tick and misclassifying the still-stranded label as a fresh crash. A prior draft removed the marker unconditionally regardless of `label_swap`'s own exit status ‚Äî the blocking finding this amendment closes.
   - **NONE** (no marker, or superseded by a later attempt) ‚Üí falls through unchanged to the pre-existing no-PR/near-success crash-declare logic.

**Numbering note**: the design doc (`docs/designs/lane-containment-gc.md` ¬ß8) drafted this as INV-112 at design time. By this PR's rebase onto `origin/main`, the head had advanced to [INV-118] (Lane-GC PR-5's guardian sidecar, #381) ‚Äî so this PR claims the next free slot, **INV-119**, per the same repo-wide "first-merged keeps it, renumber on collision" convention the prior PRs in this series already applied.

**Amendment note (2026-07-08, #441)**: the original "four INDEPENDENT admission signals" framing false-positived on large-RAM hosts with stale swap accumulation unrelated to dispatcher-managed processes ‚Äî `swap_pct` (swap-used √∑ swap-total) on a host with a comparatively small, rarely-cycled swap file can sit permanently above `GATE_SWAP_PCT` from long-running unrelated processes (editor/IDE language servers, browser automation, other agent sessions), with zero relationship to actual `MemAvailable`. Observed case: 123Gi RAM, 8Gi swap, `swap_pct=91` alongside `mem_available_mb‚âà109000` ‚Äî deferring dispatch indefinitely with no real resource constraint, recoverable only by an out-of-band operator `swapoff -a && swapon -a`. The fix is a memory-headroom rescue, not a removal of the signal: the swap signal keeps its own ceiling check but only fires when `mem_available_mb` is ALSO below `GATE_MIN_MEM_MB * GATE_SWAP_REQUIRES_MEM_MULTIPLE` (new knob, default 3 ‚Üí rescue floor 6144 MB with defaults). A signal absent/non-numeric for the rescue's own `mem_available_mb` reading fails TOWARD the pre-#441 behavior (fires) ‚Äî an unknown rescue signal is never treated as headroom. See `docs/designs/back-pressure-swap-mem-headroom-441.md` for the full false-positive mechanism, the rejected alternatives (full AND with no early-warning value; swap-velocity tracking, deferred as a future PR), and the four-band behavior table.

**INV-30 amendment**: [INV-30]'s documented verdict set (`ALIVE`/`DEAD`/indeterminate) gains a FOURTH verdict, `DEFERRED`, per this invariant. DEFERRED is distinct from indeterminate ‚Äî it is a definite verdict, never subject to [INV-30]'s ALIVE-bias or its `--at-cap` DEAD-override ‚Äî and is documented in [INV-30]'s own text as a cross-reference to this entry.

**Producer**: `skills/autonomous-dispatcher/scripts/dispatch-local.sh` (the gate: `_run_adt_gc_quick`, `_gate_check_signals`, `_admission_gate`; the `.attempt-<kind>-<N>` dispatch-attempt token, written unconditionally at the top of every invocation; **[#444]** `_gate_revert_label`, the source-level revert called immediately before `exit 75`); `lib-lane.sh` (`lane_global_live_count`, `box_health`'s macOS/BSD extension and floor-rounded `swap_pct`); `lib-dispatch.sh` (`is_dispatch_deferred_rc`, `handle_dispatch_deferred`, `_remote_pid_alive_query`'s DEFERRED parse, `pid_alive`'s side channel; **[#444]** `_local_defer_marker_verdict`/`_local_defer_marker_path`/`_revert_defer_strand`, the local-backend expiry-check + shared revert action); `liveness-check-remote-aws-ssm.sh` (the DEFERRED verdict emitter, attempt-marker freshness comparison + age-ceiling second bound); `dispatcher-tick.sh` (every `dispatch()` call site's rc=75 catch; Step 5b's DEFERRED fast-return, **[#444]** now age-bounded; **[#444]** the local-backend defer-marker check preceding the no-PR/near-success crash-declare logic).

**Consumer**: the dispatcher's own next tick (a deferred issue is picked up again via its normal selector query, local backend, or via Step 5b's fast-return leaving the active label untouched, remote backend); [INV-26] (stall decision) is unaffected ‚Äî a defer never reaches `mark_stalled`.

**Status**: **ENFORCED**, Lane-GC PR-6, closes #382. **[#444]** the source-level (A2) and tick-side defense-in-depth (B1) revert layers are additionally **ENFORCED**.

**Test**: `tests/unit/test-lane-gc-p6-gate.sh` ‚Äî the load/mem-floor/lane-cap signals each fire independently (exit 75 + defer marker + logged reason) against the REAL `dispatch-local.sh` CLI entry point, with the other three signals held healthy; a fully-healthy box proceeds to a genuine spawn; **[#441]** the swap signal's memory-headroom rescue is covered by TC-LGC6-003 (high swap + abundant memory ‚Üí rc=0, the reported false-positive fix, no defer marker), TC-LGC6-003b (high swap + mid-band memory, below the rescue floor ‚Üí rc=75, early-warning band preserved), TC-LGC6-003c (swap within limit + the same mid-band memory ‚Üí rc=0, proving the rescue's memory check doesn't engage unless swap itself is already over its ceiling), TC-LGC6-003d (high swap + non-numeric/unavailable memory signal ‚Üí rc=75, fail-toward-pre-#441-behavior when the rescue evidence itself is unknown), and TC-LGC6-003e (a malformed `GATE_SWAP_REQUIRES_MEM_MULTIPLE` operator typo falls back to the documented default rather than crashing the gate's arithmetic under `set -euo pipefail`); the pre-refusal `--quick` reclaim attempt is proven via a PATH/entry-override-shimmed `adt-gc.sh` recording exactly two invocations (the pre-existing opportunistic call plus the gate's own); re-check-once semantics are proven behaviorally (a file-based override lets the fake reclaim stub clear the injected pressure between the two checks) with an explicit counter-test proving the same scenario defers when the stub does NOT clear it; `lane_global_live_count`'s registry-driven and PID-file-fallback branches are both exercised, including the "existing but empty `lanes/` dir must use the registry path, not the fallback" edge case, AND the short-circuit-at-cap + terminal-state-skip bound (a 20-terminal + 3-live fixture asserts the probe-call count stays bounded, counted via a `lane_probe` call-counting seam); `is_dispatch_deferred_rc`/`handle_dispatch_deferred` are driven directly with stubbed collaborators proving the marker-release + label-revert and the absence of any comment/retry-counter call, PLUS a per-tick-call-site behavioral matrix (rc=1 ‚Üí marker NOT confirmed and the harness ITSELF aborts non-zero ‚Äî the restored pre-P6 abort-the-tick behavior; rc=75 ‚Üí defer path; rc=0 ‚Üí confirmed) covering all four `dispatcher-tick.sh` sites; a grep/trace pin over the extracted gate code block asserts zero kill/pkill/signal tokens (with the reclaim-step's own separately-authorized GC-enforce-mode kill capability documented, not hidden, per the honest-contract-scope amendment above); marker cleanup on the next successful dispatch is proven end-to-end; the mock-SSM DEFERRED AC drives the real `liveness-check-remote-aws-ssm.sh` against a stubbed `aws` CLI and asserts the exact two-line stdout contract and trailing-garbage-after-the-age-line degrading to indeterminate rather than a fabricated verdict; the REAL attempt-marker-vs-defer-marker comparison logic is extracted verbatim from the snippet's own `INNER_CMD` heredoc (not a re-implementation) and driven against real fixture marker files with real mtimes, covering: fresh/not-superseded ‚Üí DEFERRED; superseded (a later attempt marker) ‚Üí falls through to DEAD, never shadows; no attempt marker present (pre-upgrade host) ‚Üí the age-ceiling fallback alone gates; not-superseded but past the age ceiling ‚Üí DEAD (proving the ceiling is a genuine second bound, not merely a fallback for a missing attempt marker); `_remote_pid_alive_query`'s parse and `pid_alive`'s side-channel set/reset are driven directly; `dispatcher-tick.sh`'s Step 5b loop body is extracted (with a positive extraction-control asserting the extraction actually captured this PR's own DEFERRED-marker comment, not some unrelated span) and driven in an isolated harness proving the DEFERRED fast-return posts nothing and flips nothing, with an explicit counter-test proving the SAME harness DOES post+flip for a plain (non-DEFERRED) DEAD verdict. `tests/e2e/run-lane-gc-p6-gate-e2e.sh` (TC-LGC6-E2E-01/02) drives the REAL, unmodified `dispatch-local.sh` against a real fixture project layout end-to-end: injected pressure ‚Üí exit 75, defer marker, zero spawn; then a healthy env ‚Üí exit 0, genuine fixture-wrapper spawn, defer-marker cleanup ‚Äî the issue's stated E2E acceptance criteria, surfaced as their own CI job independent of the unit suite. **[#444]** TC-LGC6-140/141/142 drive the REAL `dispatch-local.sh` under distress with a PATH-stub `gh` recording argv, asserting exactly one `gh issue edit ‚Ä¶ --remove-label <active> --add-label <target>` per TYPE (dev-new/dev-resume/review) and that the defer marker is still written; TC-LGC6-143 is the fail-open counter-test (stub `gh` exits non-zero ‚Üí still exit 75, marker written, one WARN, no abort); TC-LGC6-144 proves idempotence ‚Äî `handle_dispatch_deferred`'s own revert running SECOND against an already-reverted label still exits 0 with its existing marker-release + label-revert + no-comment/no-retry contract intact. TC-LGC6-150/151/152/153 drive `_local_defer_marker_verdict` directly against real fixture marker files (FRESH/EXPIRED/NONE-superseded/NONE-absent); TC-LGC6-153d/e drive `_revert_defer_strand` directly against a succeeding vs. a failing `label_swap`, pinning that the function's own return code now reflects the outcome instead of a swallowed `|| true`; TC-LGC6-154/155/156/157 extract `dispatcher-tick.sh`'s Step 5 loop body (same style as TC-LGC6-110) and drive it end-to-end for the EXPIRED (reverts, no comment, no retry, marker removed), FRESH (fast-return, marker kept), and no-marker (falls through to crash-declare, proving 155/156 aren't vacuous) cases. TC-LGC6-160/161 extend the same Step 5b DEFERRED harness with an injected age at/above vs. below the threshold, proving the remote fast-return's new age bound reverts (not crashes) an expired verdict while leaving a fresh one untouched. **[review P1, #444]** TC-LGC6-158/159 are the blocking-finding regression: TC-LGC6-158 drives the SAME EXPIRED-local-marker scenario as TC-LGC6-155 but with a `label_swap` stub that FAILS, asserting the revert is attempted (no change to the no-comment/no-retry contract) but the stale marker is KEPT (not consumed) so a later tick retries ‚Äî TC-LGC6-159 is the side-by-side counter-test proving a SUCCEEDING revert still removes the marker, so 158 is genuinely gating on the revert outcome, not something else. TC-LGC6-162 is the same failure-propagation check on the remote-side edit-2 fast-return (no marker to consume there, so the assertion is narrower: still no crash comment, no retry decrement on a failed revert). All new tests (TC-LGC6-153e, 158, 159, 162) are verified to fail against the pre-fix `_revert_defer_strand` (bare `2>/dev/null || true` always returned 0, and the local-backend caller removed the marker unconditionally) and pass after. All new #444 tests overall are verified to fail against the pre-#444 code (zero `gh` invocations from the gate; `_local_defer_marker_verdict` undefined; no age check on the remote fast-return) and pass after.

**Cross-references**:
- [INV-30] (`pid_alive` authoritative under all backends) ‚Äî DEFERRED is this invariant's fourth verdict, checked and short-circuited before INV-30's own `--at-cap`/indeterminate-bias logic.
- [INV-26] (stall decision) ‚Äî a defer is invisible to `count_retries`/`mark_stalled`; never reaches this invariant's machinery.
- [INV-108] (controller-side dispatch marker) ‚Äî `handle_dispatch_deferred` releases that SAME (DIFFERENT) marker via `acquire_dispatch_marker`/`release_dispatch_marker`'s identical ownership-gated release contract, for the LOCAL-backend rc=75 catch. The remote DEFERRED plumbing's own freshness comparison does NOT read this marker (it lives on the dispatcher host, unreachable from the wrapper-host snippet) ‚Äî it reads a SEPARATE `.attempt-<kind>-<N>` token `dispatch-local.sh` writes on the wrapper host itself; the two mechanisms are related in spirit (both anchor "when was this last attempted") but are distinct artifacts on distinct hosts.
- [INV-109]/[INV-110] (lane registry, universal tagging) ‚Äî `lane_global_live_count`'s registry-driven branch is a pure read over this registry; no new state introduced.
- [INV-117] (periodic GC) ‚Äî the gate's pre-refusal reclaim attempt is the SAME `adt-gc.sh --quick` entry point the opportunistic top-of-dispatch call already invokes; this PR adds no new GC mechanism, only a second call site; that entry point's OWN enforce-mode kill authority is INV-117's, never the gate's (see the honest-contract-scope amendment above).
- `docs/designs/lane-containment-gc.md` ¬ß4-C6, ¬ß9 PR-6 ‚Äî the full gate design this invariant implements; `docs/designs/lane-gc-p6-gate.md` ‚Äî this PR's numbering re-verification, the local-backend label-revert amplification, the side-channel decision, and the wrapper-host attempt-marker freshness comparison rationale.

---

## INV-120: on Linux, when `loginctl show-user -p Linger --value` is `yes` and a probe spawn succeeds, a lane is enrolled in a `systemd-run --user --scope` unit whose cgroup membership survives re-setsid; the fast reap path escalates `systemctl --user kill` ‚Üí `cgroup.procs` emptiness ‚Üí `cgroup.kill`, and the portable pgid escalation ALWAYS also runs afterward regardless

_Triage (issue #236): [machine-checked: tests/unit/test-lane-gc-p7-scope.sh]_

**Rule** (Lane-GC series PR-7, design `docs/designs/lane-containment-gc.md` ¬ß4-C1 `_lane_backend`/¬ß4-C7, drafted as INV-114 in the design doc ‚Äî renumbered here per the numbering note below): the pgid backend (`[INV-109]`/`[INV-114]`) remains fully sufficient alone on every platform and is what macOS always uses; this invariant adds a Linux-only, feature-detected enhancement that is layered on top of it, never a replacement for it.

1. **Backend selection is probed exactly once per wrapper run, at mint time, inside `lane_install`** ‚Äî never re-probed by any kill-side consumer (`lane_kill`, the guardian, GC all read the decision back off the `lane` file's `BACKEND`/`UNIT` fields). `_lane_backend()` (`lib-lane.sh`) returns `systemd-scope` only if **every** prerequisite holds, checked cheapest-and-most-decisive first: (a) `uname -s` is `Linux`; (b) `systemd-run` is on `PATH`; (c) `loginctl show-user -p Linger --value` is exactly `yes` ‚Äî checked at **selection** time, not merely at probe time (platform:F3): without linger, `user@.service` dies with the last operator session and cascade-SIGKILLs every enrolled scope, so this is the single most consequential gate and produces the WARN a host is most likely to hit in production (this series' own dev/CI box currently reports `Linger=no`); (d) the user bus socket exists at `${XDG_RUNTIME_DIR:-/run/user/$(id -u)}/bus`; (e) a real probe spawn, `systemd-run --user --scope --quiet -- true`, actually succeeds (a degraded user manager ‚Äî e.g. failed units present ‚Äî can still fail an actual spawn even with linger=yes and a live bus). **Any single missing prerequisite is a silent, complete fallback to `BACKEND=pgid`** plus exactly one `[lib-lane] WARN:` line naming which prerequisite was missing ‚Äî never a hard failure, per the design's own principle 8 ("prevention where the platform allows it, but the portable path must be sufficient alone"). The `loginctl`/`systemd-run` probe calls are both wrapped through `_lane_bounded` (a `timeout`/`gtimeout` feature-detected wrapper, mirroring `lib-agent.sh`'s own `_AGENT_TIMEOUT_CMD` resolution) so a wedged user bus cannot hang mint-time probing on EVERY wrapper dispatch, scope-eligible or not (review round-1 finding ‚Äî verified empirically with a PATH-shimmed 60-second-sleeping `loginctl`: the probe now returns in ~5s, not 60s). A test-only `ADT_LANE_BACKEND_OVERRIDE` seam may only NARROW this function's result, never widen it: `=pgid` unconditionally forces pgid (always safe); `=systemd-scope` is a REQUEST that still has to pass every one of the five checks above ‚Äî it is not a bypass (review round-1 finding ‚Äî an inherited copy of that env var previously skipped the ENTIRE probe including the load-bearing linger gate, which would have enrolled scopes on a Linger=no host, the exact mass-SIGKILL-on-last-logout scenario this invariant exists to forbid).
2. **Scope spawn** (`lane_spawn`, backend branch): `systemd-run --user --scope --collect --quiet --unit <unit> -p TasksMax=${LANE_TASKS_MAX:-512} ${LANE_MEMORY_MAX:+-p MemoryMax=$LANE_MEMORY_MAX} -- setsid <cmd...>`. The unit name (`_lane_unit_name`) sanitizes every byte outside systemd's own safe alphabet (`[A-Za-z0-9:_.-]`) to `-` and caps the total length at 200 chars while preserving the `.<epoch>.<rand4>` uniqueness tail intact ‚Äî `PROJECT_ID` is caller-supplied (dispatcher conf, not this invariant's own control), and an unsanitized/uncapped name was verified empirically to make `systemd-run --unit` reject registration outright (`@` unconditionally rejected; unit names capped at 255 bytes total INCLUDING the `.scope` suffix systemd appends ‚Äî probed live at the exact boundary). **cgroup membership survives re-setsid** ‚Äî verified empirically on this series' own dev/CI box: a `setsid`-escaping grandchild spawned inside a scope (a different sid/pgid than the scope's own leader) remained listed in the scope's `cgroup.procs` and was reaped intact by `cgroup.kill` ‚Äî this is the one mechanism in the whole series that structurally defeats RC3 (group-escape) rather than merely detecting it after the fact. `$!` from the backgrounded `systemd-run ‚Ä¶ --scope ‚Ä¶` invocation resolves to the scope's own leader pid, which is also its pgid (`setsid` puts it in a fresh session), so the SAME `lane_record_pgid` call the pgid branch already makes is reused unconditionally ‚Äî a scope lane's leader is therefore ALSO always pgid-recorded, giving the portable path a live fallback target even if the scope machinery degrades later. **A `systemd-run` REGISTRATION failure (as opposed to a payload failure) never loses the payload**: `lane_spawn` captures `systemd-run`'s own stderr and discriminates a registration failure (systemd-run's own diagnostics always begin with a `Failed to ` line, verified present on every registration failure and absent even when the payload itself fails or writes noisy stderr) from a genuine payload failure ‚Äî only the former triggers an immediate real pgid-spawn retry, so the wrapped command is guaranteed to execute at least once regardless of which backend actually runs it (review round-1 finding ‚Äî a rejected unit name previously meant the payload was never exec'd anywhere on the host, with no error surfaced and no retry).
3. **Reap fast path** (`_lane_scope_kill`, called from both `lane_kill` and the guardian's `do_reap`, always BEFORE the pgid escalation, never instead of it): a no-op unless this lane's OWN recorded `BACKEND` is `systemd-scope` (never re-probes the host). Sequence, TERM-first per [INV-106]/[INV-114]: `systemctl --user kill -s TERM <unit>.scope` (the `.scope` suffix is REQUIRED ‚Äî `systemctl kill` assumes `.service` without it and fails to resolve the unit, verified empirically) ‚Üí bounded poll of `cgroup.procs` for emptiness across the grace window ‚Üí `echo 1 > cgroup.kill` if the feature file is present (kernel ‚â• 5.14, atomic, includes fork races) else a best-effort per-pid `KILL` loop over whatever `cgroup.procs` still lists (pre-5.14 fallback). The cgroup path is resolved via the unit's own `ControlGroup` property (`systemctl --user show -p ControlGroup --value`), not a hand-assembled `user.slice/...` path ‚Äî portable across whichever slice systemd actually placed the scope under. **The emptiness check reads `cgroup.procs`'s CONTENT, never gates on `[[ -s ]]`** ‚Äî verified empirically that `stat`-size reports 0 even while the file lists a live member pid, the identical procfs-style quirk [INV-110]'s platform:F5 already documents for `/proc/PID/environ`, confirmed here to extend to cgroupfs pseudo-files. **Both the `kill` and `show` calls are wrapped through `_lane_bounded`** (10s each) ‚Äî `lane_kill` holds `reap.lock` while this function runs, and the unconditional pgid escalation (point 4) must always still run afterward, so a wedged bus must never be able to starve it (review round-1 finding ‚Äî verified empirically with a PATH-shimmed 60-second-sleeping `systemctl`: the function now returns in ~20s, not 60s, and still degrades to rc 0).
4. **Defense in depth, unconditionally.** Every consumer of the scope fast path (`lane_kill`, the guardian's `do_reap`) runs the FULL pgid escalation afterward regardless of what the scope path did or didn't reap ‚Äî an already-collected transient unit, an unresolvable cgroup path, a missing `cgroup.kill` feature file, or a bounded-timeout degradation all degrade the scope path to a silent no-op, and the pgid escalation over the recorded `pgids` file is what still reaches the scope's own leader process in that case (point 2's "always pgid-recorded" guarantee is what makes this safe).
5. **`--doctor` extension**: alongside the existing linger check (itself now bounded via `_lane_bounded`), reports a `[ok]`/`[WARN]` line for user-bus-socket reachability (independent of linger ‚Äî a linger=yes host can still have no reachable bus) and one `backend_eligibility=<systemd-scope|pgid>` summary line that runs the real `_lane_backend()` probe live, so an operator gets the exact same answer a freshly-minted lane would get right now.

**Numbering note**: the design doc (`docs/designs/lane-containment-gc.md` ¬ß8) drafted this as INV-114 (later text refers to it as "INV-113/114 as drafted; re-verify numbering at PR-open"). By this PR's own rebase onto `origin/main`, the head had advanced past [INV-118] (Lane-GC PR-5's guardian sidecar, #381/#430) with a sibling in-flight PR in this same series (Lane-GC PR-6, back-pressure gate) independently claiming the next free slot, [INV-119] ‚Äî so this PR claims the slot after that, **INV-120**, per the same repo-wide "first-merged keeps it, renumber on collision" convention every prior PR in this series has already applied. If PR-6 does not land first, this number is re-verified (and, if necessary, renumbered again) at this PR's own open per the design's own ¬ß10 residual ("numbering re-verified at PR-open").

**Producer**: `skills/autonomous-dispatcher/scripts/lib-lane.sh` (`_lane_backend`, `_lane_warn`, `_lane_unit_name`, `_lane_cgroup_path`, `_lane_cgroup_empty`, `_lane_scope_kill`, the `lane_install` BACKEND/UNIT recording, the `lane_spawn` backend dispatch); `skills/autonomous-dispatcher/scripts/lib-guardian.sh` (`do_reap`'s `_lane_scope_kill` call, before its pgid escalation); `skills/autonomous-dispatcher/scripts/adt-gc.sh` (`--doctor`'s bus-socket check and `backend_eligibility=` line ‚Äî GC's own reap-side call into `lane_kill`, at rule 1.3, inherits the scope path automatically; no GC-decision-table code changed).

**Consumer**: `lane_kill` (every caller ‚Äî the `kill_stale_wrapper` delegate, GC rule 1.3, and any future caller ‚Äî gets the scope fast path for free); the guardian's `do_reap` ([INV-118]); `adt-gc.sh --doctor` (operator-facing eligibility diagnostic).

**Status**: **ENFORCED**, Lane-GC PR-7, closes #383.

**Test**: `tests/unit/test-lane-gc-p7-scope.sh` (64 assertions) ‚Äî backend refusal is proven TWICE: (a) REAL on this series' own Linger=no dev/CI host (`_lane_backend` genuinely returns `pgid` with a WARN naming the missing prerequisite, no override needed), and (b) via a `loginctl`/`systemd-run`/`systemctl` PATH-shim fixture asserting each individual prerequisite's refusal branch (missing `systemd-run` ‚Äî via a GENUINELY curated `PATH`, not merely a prepended decoy dir, since a host where `/bin` symlinks to `/usr/bin` defeats a simple prepend; linger=no; no bus socket; probe-spawn failure) in isolation; the OVERRIDE'S narrowing-only contract is asserted directly (`=systemd-scope` on this real Linger=no host still resolves to `pgid`; `=pgid` unconditionally forces pgid); unit-name collision test (two lanes minted in the same wall-clock second get distinct units via `lane_mint`'s own rand4) plus a long/bad-char `PROJECT_ID` case proving the sanitizer/length-cap produces a REAL-`systemd-run`-acceptable name; the registration-failure fallback is proven with a genuinely-invalid unit name ‚Äî the wrapped payload (a marker-file write) is asserted to have run via the pgid retry, exactly once (never a double-run), while a companion test proves a genuine PAYLOAD failure (successful registration) does NOT trigger a spurious retry and propagates its real exit code unchanged; the bounded-call fix is proven behaviorally against a PATH-shimmed 60-second-sleeping `loginctl`/`systemctl` (both `_lane_backend` and `_lane_scope_kill` return within their documented bound, not the full wedge duration ‚Äî and this assertion was confirmed to genuinely FAIL when the bounding fix is temporarily reverted, proving it is a real regression test); PATH-shim `systemd-run` captures argv and asserts `--unit`/`-p TasksMax=`/`--collect`/`--scope` are present and `-- setsid` ordering is preserved; PATH-shim `systemctl` captures the kill fast path's argv (`kill -s TERM <unit>.scope`, `show -p ControlGroup --value <unit>.scope`); the `lane` file records `BACKEND=systemd-scope`/`UNIT=adt-<lane_id_fs>` when a lane is DIRECTLY `lane_set` to scope post-install (never via the override, per its narrowing-only contract), and `BACKEND=pgid`/`UNIT=-` on this real host with no override; a REAL pgid-backend lane's `lane_spawn`+`lane_kill` roundtrip is unaffected byte-for-byte by this PR (regression pin, with a tripwire shim proving `systemctl`/`systemd-run` are NEVER invoked on that path); a REAL scope-backend `lane_spawn` of a `setsid`-escaping grandchild is fully reaped by `lane_kill`'s cgroup fast path on this host; mixed-fleet GC fixture (one pgid lane + one lane whose file claims `BACKEND=systemd-scope` but whose unit was never actually created) ‚Äî GC reaps both without error, the scope path degrading silently to the pgid path exactly as designed; guardian `do_reap`'s scope-branch call is proven via a grep-pin (ordered before the pgid escalation) plus the same PATH-shim argv assertions. **Honest scope note**: the PATH-shim tests prove SELECTION LOGIC and KILL-PATH COMMAND CONSTRUCTION (argv shape) only ‚Äî they do not and cannot prove real cgroup/kernel semantics (that a `setsid`-escaping process actually survives inside cgroup.procs, that `cgroup.kill` is atomic across fork races) beyond what this PR's own real (non-shimmed) scope-spawn-and-kill test demonstrates on THIS host's real kernel/systemd; a host where even that one real test cannot run (no `systemd-run`/`loginctl`/`systemctl` on `PATH` at all) skips it with an explicit reason rather than faking success. `tests/e2e/*`: no new E2E job ‚Äî see the CI-feasibility note in `docs/designs/lane-gc-p7-scope.md` (GitHub-hosted `ubuntu-latest` runners do not have `loginctl enable-linger` set and do not have a running user D-Bus session/`systemctl --user` by default in a non-login CI shell; bootstrapping one would need `sudo loginctl enable-linger` + a manual `XDG_RUNTIME_DIR` export per job, verified as a real bootstrapping cost rather than assumed) ‚Äî deferred, matching the design's own macOS-runner-deferral precedent (¬ß11 "macOS ACs decision").

**Cross-references**:
- [INV-109] (lane registry) ‚Äî `BACKEND`/`UNIT` are new fields on the SAME `lane` file this invariant defined; no new registry file, no new file format.
- [INV-106]/[INV-114] (kill-escalation contract) ‚Äî the scope fast path's own TERM-first-then-escalate shape is this same contract's cgroup-flavored instance; the pgid escalation that ALWAYS also runs afterward is literally [INV-114]'s own `_kill_group_escalate`, unchanged.
- [INV-110] (universal lane tagging) ‚Äî unaffected: the escape sweep continues to match `ADT_LANE_ID` regardless of backend; a scope lane's tag is set identically to a pgid lane's.
- [INV-117] (periodic GC) ‚Äî GC's rule 1.3 call into `lane_kill` inherits the scope fast path with zero GC-side code change, exactly as the design intended ("GC never generates wrapper exit codes at all ‚Äî it only touches DEAD-lane residue").
- [INV-118] (guardian) ‚Äî `do_reap`'s new `_lane_scope_kill` call sits immediately before its existing pgid escalation, same ordering contract as `lane_kill`'s own.
- `docs/designs/lane-containment-gc.md` ¬ß4-C1 `_lane_backend`, ¬ß4-C7, ¬ß7 platform:F3, ¬ß9 PR-7 ‚Äî the full backend-selection and scope-enrollment design this invariant implements; `docs/designs/lane-gc-p7-scope.md` ‚Äî this PR's numbering re-verification, the CI-feasibility decision, and the `cgroup.procs`-`-s`-gate finding.

## INV-121: `ISSUE_FILTER` is a conjunctive refinement over the `autonomous` baseline; multi-dispatcher slices are a pairwise-disjoint operational contract, never a code-enforced claim

_Triage (issue #236): [machine-checked: tests/unit/test-issue-filter.sh, tests/unit/test-list-selectors-terminal-defense.sh, tests/unit/test-dispatcher-tick-issue-filter.sh, tests/unit/test-multi-tick-inline-projects.sh]_

**Rule** (issue #436, design `docs/designs/issue-filter.md` ¬ß7): a dispatcher instance may scope its issue selection to a subset of open `autonomous` issues via the optional `ISSUE_FILTER` conf attribute ‚Äî a boolean expression (`and`/`or`/`not`, parentheses) over `label:<v>` / `assignee:<v>` / `assignee:none` atoms, compiled and evaluated caller-side by `lib-issue-filter.sh` (`issue_filter_compile`/`issue_filter_apply`/`issue_filter_validate`).

1. **Conjunctive refinement, never widening.** `ISSUE_FILTER` narrows the `autonomous`-labeled baseline that every selector already enumerates; it can never select an issue outside that baseline. Empty/unset filter is the identity ‚Äî jq-equal output to pre-#436 behavior at every one of the six evaluation points (`list_new_issues`, `list_pending_review`, `list_pending_dev`, `list_stale_candidates`, `list_hygiene_residue`, `count_active`).
2. **Pairwise disjointness is an OPERATIONAL contract, not code-enforced.** When multiple dispatcher instances serve one repo, their filters MUST be configured to be pairwise disjoint (no issue matches two instances' filters). The pipeline does NOT implement cross-host claiming ‚Äî the existing label state machine's swap semantics already bound the residual race to the same single-instance timing window that exists today without this feature. A violated contract (two overlapping filters) silently double-dispatches; that is an operator misconfiguration, not a code defect this invariant's tests can catch.
3. **Slice-membership stability corollary.** The disjointness contract covers not just static filter text but *mutation discipline*: routing labels / assignees on an issue currently in a transitional state (`in-progress`, `reviewing`, `pending-review`, `pending-dev`) must not be edited. Re-slicing an issue (moving it between dispatcher instances) is done only at rest (no transitional state label). Label/assignee edits are plain writes, not compare-and-swap ‚Äî a mid-tick re-route can make two instances each see the same issue in their slice for one tick; the per-controller dispatch marker ([INV-108]) does NOT dedupe across separate hosts/instances, only within one controller's own concurrent ticks.
4. **Recommended pattern**: one routing label per dispatcher instance/box (e.g. `box-tokyo`, `box-sg`), with each instance's filter anchored on `label:box-<id> and ‚Ä¶`. Documented in both `autonomous.conf.example` and `dispatcher.conf.example`.
5. **Fail-closed validation, never an unfiltered fallback.** `issue_filter_validate` runs in `dispatcher-tick.sh`'s upfront conf-validator block ‚Äî after lib sourcing, alongside `EXECUTION_BACKEND`/`REVIEW_BOTS`, and before the GH App token mint. A malformed filter, a filter referencing a reserved pipeline-state/`autonomous` label, or an assignee atom against a provider whose `.caps` lack `assignees=1` (the fail-open guard on the `(.assignees // [])` fold ‚Äî a provider that silently omits `assignees` would otherwise make every issue look unassigned and WIDEN the slice, the exact hazard this feature exists to prevent) each abort the ENTIRE tick rc‚âÝ0 with the `ADT_CFG_ISSUE_FILTER_INVALID` [INV-72] envelope ‚Äî before any token mint, Step 0 hygiene, label edit, dispatch marker, or agent spawn. **An unfiltered fallback is explicitly rejected**: it would silently violate rule 2's disjointness contract and double-dispatch against sibling instances, which is strictly worse than refusing to run the tick at all.
6. **Unmatched issues are operator-visible backlog, not an error.** An `autonomous` issue matching NO instance's filter is simply never dispatched by any instance. `status.sh` output is unaffected (it reads single issues by number, not the filtered enumeration). This is a deliberate consequence of rule 1 (refinement, not exhaustive partition) ‚Äî operators are expected to notice via ordinary issue-list review, not a pipeline alert.
7. **Per-slice hygiene consequence.** Step 0 (`list_hygiene_residue`) runs through the same `issue_filter_apply` narrowing as every other evaluation point ([INV-25] scope amendment, above) ‚Äî label residue on an issue outside every configured instance's slice is healed by whichever instance's filter happens to match it, or by the operator (a manual `gh issue edit`) if none does. This is the same "unmatched = visible backlog" consequence as rule 6, applied to hygiene specifically.
8. **`ISSUE_SCAN_LIMIT` bounds a related but distinct hazard**: filter-AFTER-limit truncation. Every enumeration site caps at `${ISSUE_SCAN_LIMIT:-100}` (unset = the pre-#436 default) BEFORE `issue_filter_apply` narrows the result ‚Äî with a filter active, the first N `autonomous` issues server-side could be mostly out-of-slice, starving this instance while matching issues sit beyond the cap. Operators configuring a non-empty `ISSUE_FILTER` on a repo whose open `autonomous` count can approach the default 100 MUST raise `ISSUE_SCAN_LIMIT` accordingly (documented in both conf examples). This is a capacity/visibility hazard bounded by a knob, not a correctness bug this invariant's tests assert against ‚Äî the tests instead assert the knob reaches every site (both `count_active` paths included).
9. **`check_deps_resolved` is deliberately NOT filtered** (design ¬ß6, non-goal). A dependency issue may live in another instance's slice, or carry no `autonomous` label at all; dependency resolution must keep the global view regardless of `ISSUE_FILTER`. This exclusion is intentional and documented here so it is never mistaken for an oversight and "fixed."

**Why**: multi-engineer repos running one dispatcher instance per box/engineer previously had no way to scope selection ‚Äî every instance selected ALL open `autonomous` issues, so two instances double-dispatched everything. `ISSUE_FILTER` gives each instance a disjoint slice via ordinary label/assignee conventions, keeping the state machine, [INV-108]'s per-controller dedup, and every other invariant unchanged for the single-instance case (empty filter = today's behavior).

**Producer**: `skills/autonomous-dispatcher/scripts/lib-issue-filter.sh` (`issue_filter_compile`, `issue_filter_apply`, `issue_filter_validate`, `issue_filter_fields`); `lib-dispatch.sh` (the six evaluation-point call sites + `${ISSUE_SCAN_LIMIT:-100}`); `dispatcher-tick.sh` (the upfront `issue_filter_validate` + `ISSUE_SCAN_LIMIT` numeric-validation precheck); `dispatcher-multi-tick.sh` (the `ISSUE_FILTER`/`ISSUE_SCAN_LIMIT` per-project export whitelist, subject to `validate_inline_block`'s existing metacharacter restriction on inline RHS values ‚Äî `$`, backtick, `;`, `&`, `|`, `\` ‚Äî documented in `dispatcher.conf.example`).

**Consumer**: every dispatcher tick, at all six evaluation points listed in rule 1; `dispatcher-tick.sh`'s upfront validator block; `dispatcher-multi-tick.sh`'s per-project subshell export.

**Status**: **ENFORCED** (issue #436, PR-B of the two-PR `ISSUE_FILTER` delivery; PR-A/#435 shipped the `assignees` provider-seam field + `assignees=1` caps bit this invariant's rule 5 capability gate reads).

**Test**: `tests/unit/test-issue-filter.sh` (compiler/apply/validate/fields-helper unit coverage, including the injection-safety atom-value-via-`--arg`-only assertions and the real degraded-fake-provider capability-gate run) ‚Äî `tests/unit/test-list-selectors-terminal-defense.sh` (TC-IFILT-100..108: filter effect at all six evaluation points, `count_active` dual-path equivalence, `ISSUE_SCAN_LIMIT` reaching every site, fail-closed enumeration on a failing leaf) ‚Äî `tests/unit/test-dispatcher-tick-issue-filter.sh` (TC-IFILT-080..087: fail-closed validation ordering before the token mint / any dispatch) ‚Äî `tests/unit/test-multi-tick-inline-projects.sh` (TC-IFILT-120..123: export propagation, unfiltered-default when absent, inline charset rejection, path-entry no-charset-restriction).

**Cross-references**:
- [INV-25] ‚Äî its heal guarantee is scoped to this instance's `ISSUE_FILTER` slice by the amendment above; this invariant's rule 7 is the operational consequence.
- [INV-72] ‚Äî the fail-closed config-class envelope contract this invariant's rule 5 relies on (`ADT_CFG_ISSUE_FILTER_INVALID` / `ADT_CFG_ISSUE_SCAN_LIMIT_INVALID`, `docs/pipeline/errors.md`).
- [INV-87] ‚Äî the provider-dispatch seam this feature's `assignees` field rides on (PR-A/#435); filter evaluation itself stays entirely caller-side, consistent with [INV-25]'s own precedent that no jq programs cross the ITP seam.
- [INV-108] ‚Äî the per-controller dispatch marker this invariant's rule 3 clarifies does NOT extend across separate dispatcher instances/hosts.
- `docs/designs/issue-filter.md` ‚Äî the full design (grammar, parser, evaluation points, multi-dispatcher contract, out-of-scope list) this invariant codifies.

## INV-122: a same-HEAD repeated E2E-gate failure (an INV-46 `fail` verdict against an UNCHANGED `(head_sha, e2e_lane_rc)` fingerprint, ‚â•`GATE_FAIL_STALL_THRESHOLD` consecutive rounds) is detected and HALTED ‚Äî the breaker transitions `reviewing ‚Üí stalled` then posts ONE structured `reason=same-head-gate-failure` report, gated on an already-`stalled` skip (deliberately NOT the dispatcher-side `may_stall_now` live-PID pre-gate ‚Äî see rationale below)

_Triage (issue #236): [machine-checked: tests/unit/test-e2e-gate-circuit-breaker.sh]_

**Rule**: the review wrapper detects a **fixed-point repetition** of the INV-46
E2E hard gate's `fail` verdict against an unchanged PR head and halts
re-dispatch automatically, instead of the dispatcher re-queuing review
indefinitely against a HEAD the dev agent never touches. The motivating case:
a downstream consumer project burned **21 review rounds** (~30 min apart, the
last 6+ byte-identical) against one commit, when the PR's preview deploy 403'd
on a missing out-of-band deploy-role grant ‚Äî every round hit the same
`_classify_e2e_gate ‚Üí fail` outcome before any review agent ever ran, so no
finding-space divergence existed for [INV-105]'s convergence breaker (a
*different* non-convergence mode: divergent findings, not fixed-point
repetition) to detect.

**Where** (single insertion point): inside `autonomous-review.sh`'s existing
`E2E_GATE == "fail"` branch, BEFORE the branch's existing
`itp_post_comment`/`emit_verdict_trailer`/`submit_request_changes`/
`itp_transition_state "reviewing" "pending-dev"` sequence ([`review-e2e-gate-fail`](state-machine.md#transition-table)).
A trip short-circuits ALL of that via an early `exit 0` ‚Äî the existing
`pending-dev` routing never runs for a tripped round.

**Distinct label movement from [INV-105]**: INV-105's breaker is dispatcher-
side (`lib-dispatch.sh`, triggered on a *completed dev session*) and moves
`pending-dev ‚Üí stalled`. This breaker is review-wrapper-side
(`autonomous-review.sh`, triggered on a *repeated gate failure*) and moves
`reviewing ‚Üí stalled` ‚Äî a genuinely new, distinct movement (declared as the
`review-e2e-gate-fail-breaker` transition in `transitions.json`), not merely a
reuse of the existing `pending-dev ‚Üí stalled` movement. Reusing the `stalled`
*label* avoids a new label-vocabulary row (and the dispatcher scan-predicate
exclusions that would ripple everywhere `stalled` is already excluded); it
does NOT avoid a new *transition* declaration ‚Äî `check-spec-drift.sh` Check
C.2 requires every write-site movement to be declared, and no pre-existing
transition declared `reviewing ‚Üí stalled`.

**Fingerprint (C1-equivalent to INV-105's frozen-head signal)**: the counter
key is `(head_sha, e2e_lane_rc)` ‚Äî NOT head alone. An outside-voice codex
review of issue #453 itself flagged that pure-head counting would misclassify
a genuinely NEW substantive bug as "stuck in a loop" if it happened to follow
an unrelated transient failure on the same untouched head:

- Same head + same rc as the immediately-prior marker ‚Üí increment count.
- Same head + a DIFFERENT rc ‚Üí reset to count=1 under the new rc (does NOT
  accumulate into the old series).
- A different head (new commit pushed) ‚Üí reset to count=1 under the new
  head/rc pair.

**State storage**: a structured HTML-comment marker posted by
`autonomous-review.sh`, following the `dispatcher-convergence-breaker` marker
convention ([INV-105]):

```
<!-- dispatcher-gate-fail-breaker: issue=<N> head=<sha> rc=<e2e_lane_rc> count=<n> -->
```

Not a label (per the movement-vs-vocabulary distinction above); not
`run-artifacts` (wrapper-side only, keyed per-run, never readable
cross-tick by the dispatcher). Marker scan is **unbounded** ‚Äî full comment
history via `itp_list_comments` (already paginates the full history) ‚Äî this
breaker cannot tolerate a miss the way `_fetch_sha_evidence`'s bounded retry
can; losing the marker on a comment-heavy issue would silently re-enable the
exact loop this invariant exists to stop.

**Threshold**: `GATE_FAIL_STALL_THRESHOLD` (default `2`), read via the same
regex-then-fallback shape as [INV-105]'s `CONVERGENCE_STALL_THRESHOLD` read,
plus an explicit floor of `>=2` (a threshold of 1 would trip on the very first
failure, defeating "repeated") and a logged warning on any fallback
(non-numeric or `<2` ‚Üí falls back to 2, with a `WARNING:` log line ‚Äî a
stricter posture than INV-105's own silent threshold fallback, per this
invariant's own testing requirements).

**The marker is computed and posted on EVERY round, not only on a trip**
(codex review [P1] finding on #453, fixed pre-merge): with the default
threshold of 2, the first failure computes count=1 (below threshold) ‚Äî if no
marker were posted on that round, the second failure would also find no
prior marker and compute count=1 again, so the breaker could never trip in
normal operation. The marker is embedded in whichever comment the round
actually posts: the trip report on a trip, or the ordinary "Review findings"
FAIL comment on a non-trip round. The marker read filters to
`authorKind != "human"` (codex review [P2] finding, fixed pre-merge ‚Äî mirrors
[INV-105]'s own marker-authenticity filter; without it, any collaborator able
to comment could pre-seed a forged marker at a high count and force the next
genuine failure to trip prematurely).

**Trip behavior**, gated in this order:

1. Check current issue labels for `stalled` FIRST ‚Äî if already stalled (e.g.
   [INV-105] tripped first for a different reason), do NOT re-trip or post a
   competing report. The structured comment body ‚Äî not the label ‚Äî is what
   distinguishes this trigger's `reason=same-head-gate-failure` from
   [INV-105]'s `reason=non-convergence` (the same reuse tradeoff INV-105
   itself already accepted for its own trigger).
2. **No `may_stall_now` call** (codex review round 2 [P1] finding, fixed
   pre-merge ‚Äî an EARLIER version of this invariant called it, mirroring
   [INV-105]'s reuse of the shared INV-26 live-PID eligibility predicate).
   `may_stall_now`'s dispatch-marker-freshness check exists so the
   *dispatcher* can ask "might some OTHER process be alive for this issue"
   before it mutates labels from outside. This breaker instead runs
   synchronously INSIDE the very review wrapper the dispatcher just
   launched ‚Äî the marker `may_stall_now` would inspect is this process's OWN
   fresh `review`-mode dispatch marker (`acquire_dispatch_marker ‚Ä¶ "review"`
   at dispatch time, held for its full TTL ‚Äî default 600s ‚Äî per
   `dispatch_marker_confirm_launched`'s own "it lives out its normal TTL"
   design), so the call would always see a fresh marker and defer,
   defeating the breaker for any E2E failure that completes within that
   window (the common case). The safety property `may_stall_now` provides
   elsewhere ‚Äî never stalling out from under a wrapper that is still alive
   and making progress ‚Äî already holds here for free: this code IS that
   wrapper, executing right now, and the `reviewing`-label single-writer
   invariant (flock-guarded PID-file guard) rules out a second one running
   concurrently.
3. **`PR_HEAD_SHA` must be non-empty** (codex review round 3 [P2] finding,
   fixed pre-merge): `PR_HEAD_SHA` is read with `|| true` and can be empty on
   a `chp_pr_view` failure. An empty head is "we don't know the head," not
   "the same head," so a fingerprint keyed on it must never satisfy the
   same-HEAD safety condition this breaker exists to enforce.
4. `itp_transition_state "$ISSUE_NUMBER" "reviewing" "stalled"` runs FIRST,
   atomically, BEFORE `RESULT_PARSED` is set ‚Äî mirrors [INV-105]'s TOCTOU fix:
   a failed transition aborts the whole wrapper under `set -euo pipefail`
   before `RESULT_PARSED` is touched, so the crash-cleanup EXIT trap correctly
   treats it as a genuine crash rather than masking a landed stall.
5. `RESULT_PARSED=true` is set IMMEDIATELY after the transition lands, BEFORE
   the report post (codex review [P1] finding, fixed pre-merge): a transient
   failure in the report post must not leave `RESULT_PARSED=false`, which
   would make the crash-cleanup EXIT trap re-add `pending-dev` on top of an
   already-landed stall.
6. Post exactly ONE structured report (marker + human-readable body,
   `reason=same-head-gate-failure`) embedding a best-effort environment-class
   classification (see below), then `exit 0` ‚Äî never reaching the normal
   `pending-dev` routing.

**Environment-class awareness (thin slice, no new error-envelope `class`)**:
reuses the existing `transient` class (`lib-error.sh`'s case-statement
already allows `config|auth|quota|transient` ‚Äî zero changes to the shared
allow-list). Registers exactly one new code,
`ADT_TRANSIENT_E2E_DEPLOY_FAIL` ([`docs/pipeline/errors.md`](errors.md#transient-class-class-transient-best-effort-not-operator-actionable)).
Because `error_surface` with `class=transient` is unconditionally log-only
(never posts, regardless of any surface override ‚Äî Clause E2), the breaker
calls `error_envelope` DIRECTLY (not `error_surface`) to render the canonical
classification text and splices it into its own report rather than relying on
`error_surface`'s posting path, which would silently swallow it. The
classification signal is local-only: `_e2e_evidence_present == 0` at the
gate's `fail` branch is the only available "E2E likely never ran"
discriminator without a new GitHub Actions API call ‚Äî `_classify_e2e_gate`
already collapses every non-zero rc to `fail` without preserving *why*, so no
rc-value-based heuristic is possible without changing that classifier (out of
scope for this invariant).

**Concurrency**: no new locking ‚Äî the existing `reviewing`-label
single-writer invariant (the flock-guarded PID-file guard) already rules out
two concurrent writers to the same issue's marker.

**Bias to MISS**: an absent, malformed, or non-matching marker all collapse to
count=0 (never a crash, never a silent inherit of an unrelated round's count).
An operator removing `stalled` WITHOUT pushing a new commit leaves the marker
still armed at (or past) `threshold - 1`; the very next same-head-same-rc
failure re-trips immediately ‚Äî documented, intentional (a new commit is the
only real reset path; matches the `stalled`-reuse contract's "removal re-arms
the pipeline" convention).

**Status**: **ENFORCED**. New `transitions.json` entry
(`review-e2e-gate-fail-breaker`, `reviewing ‚Üí stalled`) + regenerated
`state-machine.md` mermaid + prose row + `spec-guard-map.json` /
`spec-codesite-map.json` entries (`check-spec-drift.sh` Checks A/B/C all
green). Design doc: `docs/designs/issue-453-e2e-gate-circuit-breaker.md`; test
plan: `docs/test-cases/e2e-gate-circuit-breaker.md`.

**Producer**: `autonomous-review.sh` (the E2E-gate `fail` branch) + pure
helpers in `lib-review-e2e.sh` (`_gate_breaker_marker`,
`_gate_breaker_parse_count`, `_gate_breaker_next_count`,
`_gate_breaker_threshold`). Does NOT source `lib-dispatch.sh` or reuse
`may_stall_now`/`pid_alive`/`get_pid` (see the "No `may_stall_now` call"
rationale above ‚Äî an earlier version did, before a codex review round-2
[P1] finding identified why that predicate cannot apply from inside the
wrapper it would be checking).

**Tests**: `tests/unit/test-e2e-gate-circuit-breaker.sh` (fingerprint/counter/
threshold pure-logic assertions + source-of-truth wiring greps against
`autonomous-review.sh`, mirroring `test-autonomous-review-e2e-gate-open-guard.sh`'s
two-pronged style since the wrapper itself is too heavy to run end-to-end).

**See also**:
- [INV-46] ‚Äî the E2E hard gate this breaker sits inside; only its `fail`
  branch is intercepted.
- [INV-105] ‚Äî the sibling breaker whose marker/threshold/report shape this
  invariant mirrors, for a DIFFERENT trigger (divergent findings on a
  completed dev session vs. fixed-point gate-failure repetition), a
  DIFFERENT label movement (`pending-dev ‚Üí stalled` vs. `reviewing ‚Üí stalled`),
  and ‚Äî unlike INV-105 ‚Äî deliberately WITHOUT the shared `may_stall_now`
  live-PID pre-gate, which does not apply from this breaker's call site.
- [INV-72] ‚Äî the config-class error-envelope contract this invariant's
  environment-class slice deliberately does NOT extend (reuses `transient`,
  already allowed).
- [INV-127] ‚Äî the complementary non-convergence mode this invariant
  originally forward-referenced as "#449" (divergent findings / round-cap /
  severity-ratchet), now implemented as its own sibling breaker.
- `docs/designs/issue-453-e2e-gate-circuit-breaker.md` ‚Äî the full design,
  including the spec-drift implications this invariant's transition
  declaration resolves.

---

## INV-123: the completed-session `verdict=none` route is bounded ‚Äî a no-PR completed session retries via `dev-new` under `MAX_RETRIES` (Branch-C-mirror, no escalation ladder), and a PR-exists `none` (no qualifying review comment found) still fails closed to the INV-12 operator handoff

_Triage (issue #236): [machine-checked: tests/unit/test-issue-461-completed-none-bounded-retry.sh, tests/unit/test-handle-completed-routing-golden-trace.sh, tests/unit/test-mark-stalled-golden-trace.sh]_

**Rule** (issue #456/#461): `handle_completed_session_routing()`'s `none)` case is now a two-way branch keyed on whether a PR exists for the issue:

1. **PR exists** (or `fetch_pr_for_issue` fails transiently ‚Äî treated the SAME as "PR exists", fail closed, never as "no PR"): unchanged.
