opened a wrapper but never closed it (e.g. "I saw `` `reason=liveness-timeout `` mentioned somewhere, never closed") still satisfied the pattern identically to a genuine, well-formed marker/report line, reopening the SAME masking-real-progress bug via an unclosed span instead of a bare mention. Fixed by requiring BOTH delimiters of the SAME span ‚Äî `` `(TOKEN)[^`\n]*` `` for the backtick form, `` <!--[ \t]*(TOKEN)[^\n]*--> `` for the HTML-comment form, each token sub-pattern its own capturing group since Oniguruma has no branch-reset group syntax ‚Äî matching the exact producer shape (a closed, single-line span) rather than a mere look-alike opening. Every genuine producer in this codebase already emits a closed span this way, so the anchor rejects no genuine marker/report ‚Äî only bare-prose mentions and now also unclosed forgeries. **[codex review, PR #472, round 9 BLOCKING #1]** The round-8 wrapper anchor closed the bare-prose gap but left an inconsistency: `_liveness_marker_digest`/`_liveness_prior_marker` both additionally gate on `_liveness_strict_author_flag` (app-mode-only `authorKind` filter), but `_liveness_non_idempotent_count` had no such gate at all ‚Äî in `GH_AUTH_MODE=app`, a HUMAN comment that merely wraps a known token correctly (e.g. `` `reason=liveness-timeout` ``) still satisfied the anchor and was masked as idempotent, undercounting genuine human progress. The fix reclassifies (not excludes) an untrusted match: a comment counts as non-idempotent progress if its body doesn't match any known grammar, OR it matches but was posted by an `authorKind == "human"` author AND `GH_AUTH_MODE=app` ‚Äî deliberately the mirror of the digest's own gate (which excludes an untrusted match from registering *presence*; here an untrusted match must not be silently dropped from the count, it must count as real progress instead). In `GH_AUTH_MODE=token` the added disjunct is always false ‚Äî behavior is byte-for-byte unchanged, preserving the documented token-mode residual (a human posting a byte-for-byte CLOSED span identical to the genuine producer shape remains indistinguishable from the real thing in `GH_AUTH_MODE=token`, unchanged by round 10 ‚Äî the same class [INV-105]'s round-14 finding already documents). **[issue #473]** Rounds 6-10 converted the classification anchor incrementally (bare substring ‚Üí opening-wrapper ‚Üí closed-span) but never past a PER-TOKEN span ‚Äî a human comment that merely QUOTES a closed, well-formed span around a known token (e.g. "please see `` `reason=liveness-timeout` `` for context") still satisfied every round-10 anchor exactly as well as the genuine producer's own report, so it remained wrongly excluded from the count. `_LIVENESS_IDEMPOTENT_PATTERN`/`_LIVENESS_DIGEST_PATTERN` (the per-token alternations above) are REPLACED by `_LIVENESS_GRAMMARS_JSON` (`lib-liveness.sh`) ‚Äî one WHOLE-BODY regex PER PRODUCER (18 entries: the 14 breaker/self-heal grammars, the watchdog's own marker, the two liveness reports, and `no-progress-substantive-attempt:` broken out from `no-progress-substantive:` since they are genuinely distinct producer shapes), each built directly from that producer's actual `itp_post_comment` call site with every fixed word/punctuation/Markdown byte-matched and only the producer's own interpolated variables generalized to the narrowest matching character class. A comment counts as ANY known grammar ONLY when its body matches, start to end (`^...$`, with `[\s\S]*?` spanning the one genuinely free-text evidence line the two multi-line breaker reports each embed) ‚Äî a quoted, prefixed, or partially-reproduced token no longer matches, full stop, closing the residual gap every prior round narrowed but never eliminated. The accepted token-mode residual is UNCHANGED IN KIND (not newly introduced): a human posting a byte-for-byte copy of a genuine producer's ENTIRE body is still indistinguishable from the real thing in `GH_AUTH_MODE=token` ‚Äî [INV-105]'s round-14 precedent, now just anchored to the full body instead of a token span. Pinned by TC-LIVENESS-084 (`tests/unit/test-liveness-watchdog.sh`).
- `marker_digest`: a sorted, comma-joined digest of which of those SAME grammars are PRESENT ‚Äî a NEW grammar appearing is progress (a different breaker took an action) even though that same grammar's comment is excluded from the count above. Uses a SEPARATE pattern, `_LIVENESS_DIGEST_PATTERN`, that excludes the watchdog's OWN marker specifically: the watchdog posts a bare marker on essentially every evaluated tick (unconditionally on the `none` path, R3's requirement mirroring [INV-122]'s "computed and posted on EVERY round" fix), so including it in the digest would flip the digest exactly once (empty ‚Üí present) and then hold that component constant forever afterward ‚Äî a one-time false "progress" signal that then permanently pollutes the fingerprint with a component carrying no further information. This was caught and fixed during implementation (an earlier version used one shared pattern for both the count exclusion and the digest, which self-defeated: the watchdog's tick-1 marker changed the digest on tick 2, so two genuinely-identical park ticks in a row never actually produced the same fingerprint). **[round 6]** The authorKind filter (`authorKind != "human"`, mirroring [INV-105]/[INV-122]'s own marker-authenticity filter) is applied via `_liveness_strict_author_flag` ONLY when `GH_AUTH_MODE=app` ‚Äî see the counter-persistence paragraph below for the full rationale (this replaced an earlier `BOT_LOGIN`-presence check, which is never true at this call site in ANY `GH_AUTH_MODE` and so could never gate anything). **[codex review, PR #472, round 8 BLOCKING #2]** `_LIVENESS_DIGEST_PATTERN` carries the SAME wrapper anchor as `_LIVENESS_IDEMPOTENT_PATTERN`, for the identical reason: without it, a human comment merely quoting/discussing a grammar prefix in prose (e.g. "quoting the marker: dispatcher-convergence-breaker: issue=1 head=abc") would falsely register that grammar as PRESENT ‚Äî a false "progress" signal usable to reset the watchdog's clock on demand. **[codex review, PR #472, round 10 BLOCKING]** As with the count pattern above, the round-8 anchor required only the OPENING wrapper delimiter ‚Äî an unclosed backtick/HTML-comment opening (e.g. "quoting the marker: `` ` ``dispatcher-convergence-breaker: issue=1 head=abc, never closed") still falsely registered the grammar as present. Fixed identically: BOTH delimiters of the span are now required, one capturing group per alternative branch. The extraction (`_liveness_marker_digest`'s `scan()` call) was updated from `.[-1]` (which assumed a single shared capture position) to filtering the null out of each match's own 2-element result and taking the remaining (non-null) value ‚Äî since exactly one of the two alternative-specific groups is populated per match, never both. **[issue #473]** ALL LIVENESS COMMENT READS NOW USE WHOLE-BODY CANONICAL GRAMMAR MATCHING: `_LIVENESS_DIGEST_PATTERN` is likewise replaced by a `digest == true` filter over `_LIVENESS_GRAMMARS_JSON`'s per-producer whole-body entries (15 of the 18 ‚Äî every entry EXCEPT the watchdog's own marker and the two liveness reports themselves, for the identical self-referential-pollution reason above). The round-10 `scan()`/null-filtering extraction is no longer needed: whole-body match already identifies WHICH producer matched (`$g.name`), since the grammar list is keyed one-entry-per-producer rather than one-entry-per-token with shared alternation branches. The SAME first-line-forgery gap the count side closed applies here too: a comment whose FIRST LINE is a canonical marker (e.g. a well-formed `<!-- dispatcher-token: ... -->`) but whose remainder is arbitrary prose no longer registers that grammar as present. `_liveness_newest_pointer` (the tier-2 report's best-effort "newest session report / verdict" DISPLAY pointer) is explicitly OUT OF SCOPE and remains an unanchored substring test ‚Äî it gates no progress/fingerprint/authenticity decision, sources from arbitrary agent/review-wrapper free text with no canonical whole-body shape to anchor against, and a false match there degrades a display hint, never INV-128's actual guarantees; see that function's own docstring (`lib-liveness.sh`) for the full audit rationale. Pinned by TC-LIVENESS-085, plus TC-LIVENESS-086..094 (full-body fixture coverage ‚Äî including `dispatcher-gate-fail-breaker:`, previously untested against its real producer text ‚Äî for the 9 `_LIVENESS_GRAMMARS_JSON` entries TC-LIVENESS-006..085 exercised only indirectly).

Fingerprint unchanged across a tick = a **no-op tick**.

**Counter + tier-1 latch persistence**: `<!-- dispatcher-liveness-watchdog: issue=<N> fingerprint=<hash> count=<n> tier1=<0|1> tripped=<0|1> -->`, posted/updated on EVERY evaluated tick (mirrors [INV-122]'s "computed and posted on EVERY round, not only on a trip" requirement ‚Äî without this, the very first no-op tick would leave no marker for the second to find, and the counter could never advance in normal operation). **[round 6]** The marker is ALWAYS posted as its OWN bare comment ‚Äî the tier-1/tier-2 human-readable reports (below) never embed it as their first line. **[round 8]** `tripped` is a FIELD ADDED to the marker, `1` on the marker posted as part of a tier-2 transition and `0` on every other tick ‚Äî see the cutoff paragraph below for why. Read back via `_liveness_prior_marker` (`lib-liveness.sh`) ‚Äî the LAST comment STRUCTURALLY anchored on the marker's own exact grammar, a WHOLE-BODY anchor (`^<!-- dispatcher-liveness-watchdog: ... -->[[:space:]]*$`, tolerating only trailing whitespace ‚Äî a genuine marker's entire body is always just the marker) that falls STRICTLY AFTER the cutoff (see the cutoff paragraph below). Fingerprint match ‚Üí `count = min(stored.count + 1, stall_ticks)` (capped ‚Äî see the count-cap paragraph below), `tier1 = stored.tier1` (latch persists); fingerprint mismatch ‚Üí `count = 1`, `tier1 = 0` (full reset ‚Äî a genuinely new episode gets a fresh tier-1 warning even on a head/label that previously tripped tier 1 before recovering). A malformed, absent, or non-matching marker collapses to `count=0` (bias to MISS, never a crash).

**[operator guidance, PR #472 round 6] Authenticity: structural anchor (all modes) + `authorKind` gate (app mode only)**. Two BLOCKING findings across rounds 2 and 5 pulled in opposite directions and cannot both be satisfied by tightening/loosening one unconditional `authorKind` gate: round 2 proved an unconditional `authorKind != "human"` gate permanently zeroes the counter in `GH_AUTH_MODE=token` (this marker is posted AND read back inside the SAME dispatcher process ‚Äî `dispatcher-tick.sh` ‚Üí `lib-dispatch.sh` ‚Äî which, like [INV-105]'s `_frozen_convergence_rounds_json`, NEVER resolves `BOT_LOGIN`, so the dispatcher's own genuine marker normalizes to `authorKind=human` there); round 5 proved that with NO `authorKind` gate at all, any collaborator can post a bare forged marker and force an immediate tier-2 trip. The resolution ‚Äî the SAME two-part pattern `classify_recent_review_verdict` already establishes ([#389]/[#393]) ‚Äî is `_liveness_strict_author_flag` (`lib-liveness.sh`): apply the `authorKind != "human"` filter ONLY when `GH_AUTH_MODE=app` (the genuine wrapper posts under a GitHub App identity there ‚Äî REST-derived `user.type == "Bot"`, independent of whether `BOT_LOGIN` itself is ever resolved); in the default/token mode, rely on the whole-body structural anchor alone. The accepted token-mode residual ‚Äî a human posting a byte-for-byte copy of the bare marker as their ENTIRE comment ‚Äî is the same documented, accepted exposure every other structural-only anchor in this codebase carries (see [INV-105]'s round-14 finding), now bounded in blast radius by the count cap (below) rather than left unbounded. `_liveness_marker_digest` uses the SAME flag for its own authorKind gate over the sibling breaker/self-heal marker grammars.

**[operator guidance, PR #472 round 6] Count cap ‚Äî defense in depth, not a substitute for authentication**: `_liveness_next_count` accepts an optional `stall_ticks` argument and caps its result there. This does NOT close the token-mode residual above (a forger who can already construct a fingerprint-matching bare marker can simply choose `count = stall_ticks - 1` and force a trip on the very next genuine no-op tick regardless of any cap) ‚Äî it bounds the BLAST RADIUS of a forged absurd count (e.g. `count=999999999`): capped, such a value can propagate no further than `stall_ticks` into the tier-action decision and the operator-facing report text, rather than appearing verbatim in an alarming, meaningless tick-count string. The one production call site (`_liveness_evaluate_issue`) always supplies `stall_ticks`; the cap only ever applies to an ALREADY fingerprint-matching series (the anchor + fingerprint-match gate runs first, unconditionally) ‚Äî a fingerprint-mismatched forged marker resets to `count=1` regardless of the forged value, cap or no cap.

**Why the prior-marker read needs a cutoff, not just a structural anchor** ([codex review, PR #472, BLOCKING #2]; ordering hardened round 6; cutoff detection anchored round 7; cutoff signal REDESIGNED round 8): a bare "last structurally-anchored marker" scan (no cutoff) would, on the tick immediately after an operator fixes the underlying park and removes `stalled` (re-arming via Step 2) with the fingerprint's OTHER components otherwise unchanged, read an OLD trip marker back ‚Äî high `count`, `tier1=1` ‚Äî and immediately re-trip tier 2 again instead of starting a fresh liveness episode. `_liveness_prior_marker` fixes this with the SAME cutoff-then-scan shape [INV-127]'s `_review_cap_prior_marker` already established (itself mirroring [INV-05]'s "Marking as stalled" cutoff).

**[codex review, PR #472, round 8 BLOCKING #1]** cutoff = the latest qualifying marker's `createdAt` where that marker's OWN `tripped` field equals `1`, or the epoch if no trip has ever fired; scan for the marker-anchor match STRICTLY AFTER that cutoff (`>`, not `>=`). This is the THIRD iteration of the cutoff mechanism, and the round-8 change is structural rather than incremental: rounds 3 and 6 anchored the cutoff to a SEPARATE, hand-typed heading-text pattern (`_LIVENESS_TIER2_HEADING`) via `contains("Liveness watchdog tripped")`; round 7 found that unanchored substring test let ANY comment merely mentioning the bare phrase anywhere in its body register as a forged trip, and tightened it to `startswith($heading)` (the comment's body must OPEN with the report's exact heading line). Round 8 found `startswith()` was STILL forgeable in the default `GH_AUTH_MODE=token` topology: an unauthenticated comment that simply OPENS with the exact heading line (trivial for a human to copy) satisfies the anchor identically to the genuine report, moving the cutoff forward, excluding the real earlier marker, and permanently resetting a still-frozen issue's series to `count=1` ‚Äî the SAME "real park dodges tier 2 indefinitely" failure mode round 7 also fixed one instance of. Each of rounds 3/6/7 patched the SAME underlying design flaw ‚Äî a second, independently-typed free-text pattern carries no authentication of its own, no matter how tightly it is anchored ‚Äî without addressing it; round 6‚Üí7's own gap (marker anchor tightened to whole-body while the cutoff detector's match stayed on a separately-typed, less-tightly-anchored string) is direct evidence the two mechanisms drift apart when they are not the same mechanism. The round-8 fix eliminates the free-text cutoff signal ENTIRELY: `tripped` is a FIELD on the marker's own already-authenticated, whole-body-anchored grammar, so the cutoff computation and the prior-marker scan it feeds share the identical anchor and the identical `authorKind` filter ‚Äî there is no second pattern left to drift out of sync with, or for a human to forge independently of the marker itself. `_LIVENESS_TIER2_HEADING` remains as the tier-2 report's operator-facing display heading only (rendered by the TIER2REPORT heredoc); no detector reads it anymore. Since the marker is a comment DISTINCT from (and posted STRICTLY BEFORE) the trip report, both the marker's `tripped=1` field and its `createdAt` are set/committed atomically with the tier-2 transition, before the report ‚Äî see the tier-2 bullet below. The `_liveness_strict_author_flag`-gated authenticity filter is applied to both the cutoff computation and the final scan from the SAME filtered row set, so they can never disagree on which comments are eligible.

**Eligibility pre-gate (exemptions, in order ‚Äî never a false-positive no-op)**:

1. `was_just_dispatched` ‚Äî [INV-09] JUST_DISPATCHED skip.
2. `is_within_grace_period` ‚Äî [INV-18] dispatch-marker TTL; a fresh unconsumed dispatch marker exempts the tick.
3. `_liveness_wrapper_alive(kind, issue)` ‚Äî composes `_dispatch_marker_recent` + `pid_alive <kind> <issue>`, the SAME two primitives [INV-26]'s shared `may_stall_now` uses, parameterized by which wrapper kind is relevant to the label actually being evaluated (`issue` for `pending-dev`, `review` for `pending-review`) ‚Äî never a new liveness check.
4. `itp_list_comments` transient failure ‚Üí skip this tick entirely (fail-toward-defer, mirrors [INV-125]'s Part-2 preflight fix ‚Äî an empty/failed read must never be misread as "no comments exist," which would corrupt the fingerprint's comment-count component).
5. **[codex review, PR #472, round 8 BLOCKING #3]** `fetch_pr_for_issue` transport failure ‚Üí skip this tick entirely, the SAME fail-toward-defer posture as exemption 4. `resolve_pr_for_issue`/`chp_find_pr_for_issue` are fail-closed on a `gh` transport error (nonzero rc); only rc=0 with an empty result means "genuinely no PR bound to this issue." The pre-fix code checked ONLY for a non-empty result (`|| pr_info=""` collapsed both outcomes), so a transient API blip on an issue that DOES have a PR silently reset the counter instead of deferring ‚Äî masking a park exactly on the tick meant to detect it.

**Two-tier terminal action (pure decision function)**: `_liveness_tier_action(count, tier1, notice, stall)` ‚Äî `count >= stall` ‚Üí `tier2` (unconditional once the count threshold is met; NOT gated on tier 1 having fired, so a misconfigured/skipped tier-1 never blocks the terminal bound); else `count >= notice AND tier1 == 0` ‚Üí `tier1`; else `none`.

- **Tier 1** (`count >= LIVENESS_NOTICE_TICKS`, tier1 not yet latched): posts the bare marker (with `tier1=1`, `tripped=0`) as its OWN comment, THEN a SEPARATE non-idempotent comment ‚Äî "no observable progress for N ticks" + the fingerprint components (label, PR head, non-idempotent comment count, marker digest) + `@REPO_OWNER`. The marker sets `tier1=1`, so it does not re-fire while the fingerprint stays unchanged (R3). The report text is itself excluded from the fingerprint's progress count (`reason=liveness-no-progress` is one of `_LIVENESS_GRAMMARS_JSON`'s whole-body entries, [round 6], [issue #473]) ‚Äî otherwise the tier-1 post would immediately register as "a new comment" and defeat its own counter on the very next tick.
- **Tier 2** (`count >= LIVENESS_STALL_TICKS`): re-checks the issue's CURRENT labels for `stalled` immediately before acting (mirrors [INV-122]'s `_gf_already_stalled` check) ‚Äî a specific breaker may have won the race between the candidate-list fetch and this evaluation; if so, skip with no competing report. Otherwise: `label_swap <issue> <active_label> stalled` runs FIRST, atomically (mirrors [INV-105]/[INV-122]'s TOCTOU fix ‚Äî a failed transition aborts under `set -euo pipefail` before either comment is posted, so the next tick re-evaluates from scratch rather than leaving an orphan report/marker against a still-non-stalled issue), THEN posts the bare marker (with `tripped=1`, [round 8]) as its OWN comment, THEN a SEPARATE structured `reason=liveness-timeout` report: last-known fingerprint, tick counts (`count`/`notice_threshold`/`stall_threshold`), and a pointer to the newest session-report/verdict comment (`_liveness_newest_pointer`, best-effort, `(none found)` when absent). Posting the marker BEFORE the report (never the reverse, and never embedded ‚Äî [round 6]) is still done for operator-facing readability, but is no longer LOAD-BEARING for the cutoff's correctness the way it was pre-round-8 ‚Äî the cutoff now keys on the marker's own `tripped` field, not on post order or timestamp coincidence with the report.

**Idempotency**: the marker's `{fingerprint, count, tier1}` triple IS the state; a re-run against an unchanged fingerprint with `tier1` already latched at 1 takes the `none` action (bare marker refresh only) rather than re-posting tier 1, and tier 2 fires at most once per fingerprint episode because the very next tick after the `stalled` transition finds the issue excluded from `list_pending_dev`/`list_pending_review` entirely.

**[codex review, PR #472, round 9 BLOCKING #2] Write-failure resilience ‚Äî marker/report posts retry, never silently swallow**: every prior round posted the marker and the tier-1/tier-2 report via a bare `itp_post_comment ... 2>/dev/null || true` ‚Äî appropriate for a comment that merely informs a human, wrong for the MARKER specifically, since it IS the counter/tier1-latch/`tripped`-field persistence mechanism (R4/D2). A silently lost marker write means the very next tick's `_liveness_prior_marker` finds nothing, `_liveness_next_count` resets to `count=1` under an otherwise-unchanged fingerprint, and the series can never advance past that reset ‚Äî a self-inflicted instance of the exact "permanent silent park" bug class this watchdog exists to close. Losing a `tripped=1` marker specifically is worse: a later resume tick's cutoff computation finds no qualifying `tripped=1` marker, falls back to the epoch, and reads the OLD pre-trip marker back as "prior" ‚Äî reproducing the round-3/6/7/8 immediate-re-trip-on-resume bug via a lost write rather than a design gap. `_liveness_post_marker`/`_liveness_post_report` (`lib-dispatch.sh`) fix this: each retries the post exactly once (mirrors the established INV-85 retry-then-notice shape already used for the `no-progress-substantive-attempt:<head>` marker at the same file's INV-35 branch). `_liveness_post_marker` additionally posts a LOUD best-effort `@REPO_OWNER` operator notice if BOTH attempts fail, and returns 1 so the caller can skip a dependent report post ‚Äî the `tier1` branch does exactly this (`_liveness_post_marker ... || return 0`): if the marker itself never lands, posting the human-readable escalation anyway would just repeat the same prose every tick while the marker keeps failing to write, since the counter state never actually advanced. The `tier2` branch does NOT gate its report post on the marker's success ‚Äî `label_swap` already committed the irreversible `stalled` transition before either post is attempted, so the report is the operator's one chance at an explanation; it is posted best-effort regardless, via `_liveness_post_report`'s own independent retry. `_liveness_post_report` does not escalate as loudly as the marker helper ‚Äî a lost report costs only operator-facing prose, since the counter/tier1-latch/`tripped` state is carried entirely by the (independently retried) marker, posted first in both tier branches.

**[codex review, PR #472, round 9 follow-up self-review] `|| true` is mandatory at every non-gating call site ‚Äî a bare call trips `set -e` and aborts the WHOLE tick.** Both `lib-dispatch.sh` and `dispatcher-tick.sh` run under `set -euo pipefail`. `_liveness_post_marker`/`_liveness_post_report` deliberately CAN return 1 (so the `tier1` branch can detect persistent marker failure and short-circuit its report) ‚Äî but a BARE call to either (no `&&`/`||`/`if` around it) trips `set -e` on that non-zero return, aborting `_liveness_evaluate_issue`, which then propagates through `run_liveness_watchdog`'s loop and aborts the entire dispatcher tick before the metrics-prune tail. An initial version of this fix introduced exactly this regression: the `none` branch's marker post, the `tier1` branch's report post, and the `tier2` branch's marker AND report posts were all left as bare calls. Every one of those four call sites now ends in `|| true` ‚Äî restoring the pre-round-9 never-abort-the-tick guarantee while still getting the retry benefit inside the helper. The ONE exception is the `tier1` branch's marker post, which is intentionally `|| return 0` (a `set -e`-exempt context) ‚Äî that IS the load-bearing short-circuit, not a bug. Pinned by TC-LIVENESS-075..078, which spawn a real `bash -euo pipefail` subshell (the existing test harness sources everything under `set +e`, which cannot detect this class of regression at all ‚Äî every bare call looks "safe" there regardless of whether `|| true` is present).

**Bias to MISS**: a live wrapper, a fresh dispatch marker, a `JUST_DISPATCHED` issue, or any fingerprint change all suspend/reset counting ‚Äî never a false escalation on a legitimate wait. An operator can reset the clock by causing ANY observable change (a comment, a label edit, a push) ‚Äî the same "removal re-arms" posture [INV-05]/[INV-105] already establish. Idempotent notices from OTHER breakers never count as progress (R1); a human comment whose body does not structurally match the marker's exact WHOLE-BODY anchored grammar (e.g. discussing, partially quoting it, or appending trailing content) is rejected and the genuine counter is unaffected ‚Äî it simply re-reads as "no authentic prior marker" and restarts at `count=1`. A human posting a byte-for-byte copy of the genuine bare marker as their entire comment is the one documented, accepted residual exposure in `GH_AUTH_MODE=token` (same class as [INV-105]'s round-14 finding; closed in `GH_AUTH_MODE=app` by the `authorKind` gate above). **[round 8/9]** This residual now has TWO directions: a forged HIGH-count marker still TRIGGERS an early tier action (bounded by the count cap, TC-LIVENESS-056..058), and ‚Äî since `tripped` moved onto the marker itself ‚Äî a forged `tripped=1` marker can also SUPPRESS an in-progress series (TC-LIVENESS-065..066). The trigger direction is bounded by the count cap; the suppression direction is self-limiting instead (the genuine watchdog re-posts its own marker the very next tick and resumes counting, so indefinite suppression requires the forger to keep posting a fresh, audit-visible forgery roughly every `stall_ticks`) rather than capped.

**Producer**: `lib-liveness.sh` ‚Äî pure helpers only (`_liveness_fingerprint`, `_liveness_canonical`, `_liveness_non_idempotent_count`, `_liveness_marker_digest`, `_liveness_marker`, `_liveness_parse_marker`, `_liveness_prior_marker`, `_liveness_next_count`, `_liveness_next_tier1`, `_liveness_tier_action`, `_liveness_notice_ticks`, `_liveness_stall_ticks`, `_liveness_watchdog_enabled`, `_liveness_wrapper_alive`, `_liveness_newest_pointer`, `_liveness_strict_author_flag`, no I/O) plus `_LIVENESS_TIER2_HEADING` (display text only as of [round 8] ‚Äî no detector reads it) and, as of [issue #473], the whole-body-per-producer `_LIVENESS_GRAMMARS_JSON` array (which REPLACED the wrapper-anchored `_LIVENESS_IDEMPOTENT_PATTERN`/`_LIVENESS_DIGEST_PATTERN` constants built from the shared `_LIVENESS_MARKER_TOKENS` alternation, [round 8]). `lib-dispatch.sh` ‚Äî the orchestration (`_liveness_evaluate_issue`, `run_liveness_watchdog`), the single call site for the tier-2 `label_swap` AND (round 8) the single call site distinguishing a `fetch_pr_for_issue` transport failure from a genuine "no PR", plus (round 9) the retry-and-notice write wrappers `_liveness_post_marker`/`_liveness_post_report`. `dispatcher-tick.sh` Step 6 is the single call site for `run_liveness_watchdog`.
**Consumer**: the operator (tier-1 `@REPO_OWNER` mention, tier-2 report); the pipeline's own selectors (`list_pending_dev`/`list_pending_review` naturally exclude a watchdog-stalled issue on the next tick, same as any other `stalled` issue).
**Status**: **ENFORCED** (closes #467). New `transitions.json` entries (`liveness-watchdog-stall-pending-dev` `pending_dev ‚Üí stalled`, `liveness-watchdog-stall-pending-review` `pending_review ‚Üí stalled` ‚Äî the latter is a genuinely NEW movement, no prior transition declared `pending_review ‚Üí stalled`) + regenerated `state-machine.md` mermaid + `spec-guard-map.json`/`spec-codesite-map.json` entries (`check-spec-drift.sh` Checks A/B/C all green). `LIVENESS_NOTICE_TICKS`/`LIVENESS_STALL_TICKS`/`LIVENESS_WATCHDOG_ENABLED` documented in `autonomous.conf.example`. Design doc: `docs/designs/issue-467-liveness-watchdog.md`; test plan: `docs/test-cases/liveness-watchdog.md`.

**Config validation**: `LIVENESS_NOTICE_TICKS` (default 6, floor `>=2`) and `LIVENESS_STALL_TICKS` (default 18, floor `>=2`, additionally must be `> LIVENESS_NOTICE_TICKS`), both read with the same regex-then-fallback-with-warning shape as [INV-122]'s `_gate_breaker_threshold`: the warning goes to stderr ONLY, never through `log()`, since every call site captures the function's stdout via `$(...)` for the numeric result (the codex [P2] fix on #453, mirrored here). [codex review, PR #472, BLOCKING] When the configured pair fails the relative constraint (e.g. `6/6`), `LIVENESS_STALL_TICKS` falls back to its own default (18) rather than unconditionally clamping to `notice+1` ‚Äî a misconfigured pair is a config error, and clamping to `notice+1` would silently turn a typo into an aggressive false-stall path for otherwise-legitimate slow waits. The one exception: if `LIVENESS_NOTICE_TICKS` is itself validly configured `>= 18`, the default 18 would still fail `stall > notice`, so the fallback escalates to `notice+1` in that case only ‚Äî the function never returns an inverted pair to a caller. `LIVENESS_WATCHDOG_ENABLED` (default `true`) is an escape hatch: `false` skips Step 6 entirely (no fingerprinting, no marker reads/writes, `list_pending_dev`/`list_pending_review` are never even called for this purpose). **[codex review, PR #472, round 8 BLOCKING #4]** `run_liveness_watchdog` ‚Äî the ONE production call site for both threshold readers ‚Äî captures each reader's stderr via a `mktemp` file (mirroring `ci_is_green`'s own capture-then-relog pattern, `lib-dispatch.sh` Step 5a) and re-emits any non-empty WARNING through `log()`, rather than redirecting it straight to `/dev/null`. The fallback behavior itself is unchanged either way (a misconfigured value still correctly falls back to its default) ‚Äî pre-round-8, that fallback happened SILENTLY, with no trace in the dispatcher's own log output, making a `LIVENESS_NOTICE_TICKS`/`LIVENESS_STALL_TICKS` typo far harder to diagnose on a real run than R5 intends.

**Tests**: `tests/unit/test-liveness-watchdog.sh` ‚Äî TC-LIVENESS-001..008 (fingerprint component sensitivity, idempotent-notice exclusion, self-marker exclusion from both the count and the digest), TC-LIVENESS-009..015 (counter/tier1-latch round-trip through the marker grammar, malformed-marker bias-to-MISS), TC-LIVENESS-016..022 (tier-action pure decision function, including tier-2 firing unconditionally even when tier-1 was skipped), TC-LIVENESS-023..030 (threshold validation/fallback/warning-to-stderr-only, the relative `stall > notice` constraint falling back to the default rather than a `notice+1` clamp, `LIVENESS_WATCHDOG_ENABLED=false`), TC-LIVENESS-031..037 (all four exemptions, plus the structural terminal-label exclusion via the existing selectors), TC-LIVENESS-038..044 (the full two-tier sequence via `_liveness_evaluate_issue` driven tick-by-tick: tier-1 exactly once at the notice threshold ‚Äî now pinned as a comment DISTINCT from its marker (TC-038c/d) ‚Äî no re-fire, tier-2 exactly once at the stall threshold with the atomic transition-then-report ordering and the SAME marker/report split with post-ORDER pinned (TC-040e/f/g), a mid-sequence fingerprint change causing a full reset with no tier-2 reached on the interrupted series, the already-stalled race skip, and a structurally-non-matching human comment being rejected by the marker's anchored-grammar readback even with `BOT_LOGIN` unset ‚Äî the real `GH_AUTH_MODE=token` topology), TC-LIVENESS-046..050 ([codex review, PR #472, BLOCKING #2, then round 8's `tripped`-field redesign] `_liveness_prior_marker`'s cutoff-then-scan behavior against constructed fixtures modeling the real marker-then-report producer shape: the `tripped=1` marker itself is excluded right after a trip, a genuinely later post-resume marker qualifies and continues a fresh series, the no-trip-yet case is unaffected, the full `_liveness_evaluate_issue` re-arm sequence does not immediately re-transition to `stalled`, and a SECOND trip-resume cycle's cutoff tracks the latest `tripped=1` marker rather than the first), TC-LIVENESS-051..058 ([operator guidance, round 6] the whole-body anchor rejecting a marker-plus-trailing-prose shape while still accepting trailing whitespace, `GH_AUTH_MODE=app`'s `authorKind` gate rejecting a human forgery while accepting a bot/App-authored bare marker (with the token-mode-unchanged pin from TC-044c/d as the regression guard against re-coupling the two), `_liveness_strict_author_flag` itself across all three `GH_AUTH_MODE` values, the count cap capping an absurd stored count at `stall_ticks` while leaving an uncapped call and a below-cap count untouched, and an end-to-end proof that the cap never substitutes for the fingerprint-match gate ‚Äî a fingerprint-mismatched forged marker still resets to `count=1`), TC-LIVENESS-059 ([codex review, PR #472, rounds 7/8 regression pin] NEITHER a bare mid-comment mention of the tier-2 heading NOR a comment that merely OPENS with the exact heading text acts as a forged cutoff anymore ‚Äî the genuine earlier marker still qualifies as the prior marker regardless of either historical forgery shape, now that the cutoff no longer reads free text at all), TC-LIVENESS-060..062 ([codex review, PR #472, round 8 BLOCKING #2] the wrapper-anchored `_LIVENESS_IDEMPOTENT_PATTERN`/`_LIVENESS_DIGEST_PATTERN`: a bare mid-prose token mention neither reduces the non-idempotent count nor registers in the digest, while the SAME token backtick/HTML-comment-wrapped does both, including through the `no-progress-substantive(?:-attempt)?:` inner-optional-group case), TC-LIVENESS-063..064 ([codex review, PR #472, round 8 BLOCKING #3] a `fetch_pr_for_issue` transport failure defers the entire tick with zero comment posts, while a genuinely-empty rc=0 result still proceeds and posts the bare marker), TC-LIVENESS-065..066 ([round 9, independent self-review gap] the `tripped` field's OWN forgery-SUPPRESSION direction, distinct from the count cap's already-pinned forgery-TRIGGER direction: in `GH_AUTH_MODE=token` a forged human `tripped=1` marker DOES suppress a genuine bot in-progress marker ‚Äî the documented token-mode residual, unchanged in kind from before round 8, just relocated onto the new field ‚Äî while in `GH_AUTH_MODE=app` the SAME forgery is rejected and the genuine marker still qualifies), TC-LIVENESS-067..069 ([codex review, PR #472, round 9 BLOCKING #1] `_liveness_non_idempotent_count` gains the SAME app-mode-only `authorKind` gate the digest/marker reads already have: in `GH_AUTH_MODE=app` a human's wrapped-token comment now counts as genuine progress instead of being masked as idempotent, the SAME wrapped token from a bot/App identity is still excluded, and `GH_AUTH_MODE=token` behavior is byte-for-byte unchanged), TC-LIVENESS-070..074 ([codex review, PR #472, round 9 BLOCKING #2] `_liveness_post_marker`/`_liveness_post_report`'s retry-then-notice behavior: a first-attempt success posts exactly once with no WARNING, a fail-then-succeed retry posts exactly twice with no operator notice, persistent failure posts a WARNING plus ‚Äî for the marker helper only ‚Äî a THIRD loud `@REPO_OWNER` notice call, and an end-to-end proof through `_liveness_evaluate_issue` that a persistently-failing tier-1 marker write short-circuits the dependent report post entirely, attempting the marker write twice with zero report posts), TC-LIVENESS-075..078 ([codex review, PR #472, round 9 follow-up self-review] each spawns a REAL `bash -euo pipefail` subshell ‚Äî the ONLY way to catch a bare-call-trips-`set -e`-and-aborts-the-tick regression, since the surrounding test harness sources everything under `set +e`: a persistently-failing marker write in the `none`/`tier2` branches, and a persistently-failing report write (with the marker itself succeeding, via a seeded prior marker so the evaluation actually reaches the `tier1`/`tier2` branch) in the `tier1`/`tier2` branches, all reach the end of `_liveness_evaluate_issue` normally rather than aborting), TC-LIVENESS-079..083 ([codex review, PR #472, round 10 BLOCKING] the closed-span wrapper anchor: an UNCLOSED backtick span or an UNCLOSED HTML-comment opening neither reduces the non-idempotent count nor registers in the digest (TC-079a/b, TC-080a/b), a backtick span that only "closes" after an embedded newline (not a valid single-line Markdown code span) is still rejected (TC-081), a genuinely CLOSED span of either form still behaves exactly as it did pre-round-10 (TC-082a/b), and the two-independent-capturing-groups extraction still correctly joins multiple distinct grammars present across separate comments (TC-083)). `tests/e2e/run-liveness-watchdog-e2e.sh` ‚Äî TC-LIVENESS-045 (a stub-dispatcher replay of the [INV-125] `api_error` park shape ‚Äî `pending-dev`, a spent crashed-session-retry budget, a dead wrapper, a frozen fingerprint ‚Äî run tick-by-tick for 25 ticks: tier-1 at exactly tick 6, tier-2 at exactly tick 18, each exactly once, the marker/report split holding through the full replay (TC-045i/j), post-stall ticks are complete no-ops, an operator-resume tick after the tier-2 report restarts the count at 1 instead of re-tripping, plus a source-of-truth grep pinning Step 6's placement in `dispatcher-tick.sh` after Step 5 and before the metrics-prune tail), TC-LIVENESS-045k ([round 7, fixture updated round 8] the SAME forged-mid-comment-mention regression driven through the real `_liveness_evaluate_issue` end-to-end on a fresh in-progress episode: the count continues incrementing (10 ‚Üí 11) across the forged comment rather than resetting to 1).

**Guards preserved**: [INV-105]/[INV-111]/[INV-122]/[INV-123]/[INV-125] are UNTOUCHED ‚Äî no change to any existing breaker's threshold, fingerprint, or marker; they remain the faster, better-diagnosed specific paths for their own park shapes, firing (and resolving the park) well before this invariant's own thresholds would ever be reached in the ordinary case. [INV-91] (all I/O through existing `itp_*` verbs ‚Äî `itp_list_comments`, `itp_post_comment`, `itp_read_task`; zero raw `gh`). [INV-26] (no new liveness predicate ‚Äî `_liveness_wrapper_alive` composes the same two primitives `may_stall_now` does). [INV-09]/[INV-18] (the same JUST_DISPATCHED/grace-period exemptions every other selector honors).

**Cross-references**:
- [INV-105](#inv-105-a-non-converging-devreview-loop-a-failed-substantivedev-actionabletrue-verdict-that-churns-n-completed-zero-commit-dev-resume-rounds-against-a-frozen-pr-head-is-detected-and-halted--the-breaker-transitions-to-stalled-then-posts-one-structured-reasonnon-convergence-report-gated-behind-the-shared-may_stall_now-live-pid-pre-gate-idempotent-per-issue-head-trailer-hash-session_id) ‚Äî the marker/threshold/report shape this invariant's tier-2 mirrors, for the FIRST of the five point-fixes this closes the class of.
- [INV-122](#inv-122-a-same-head-repeated-e2e-gate-failure-an-inv-46-fail-verdict-against-an-unchanged-head_sha-e2e_lane_rc-fingerprint-gate_fail_stall_threshold-consecutive-rounds-is-detected-and-halted--the-breaker-transitions-reviewing--stalled-then-posts-one-structured-reasonsame-head-gate-failure-report-gated-on-an-already-stalled-skip-deliberately-not-the-dispatcher-side-may_stall_now-live-pid-pre-gate--see-rationale-below) ‚Äî the threshold-validation shape (regex-then-fallback-with-stderr-only-warning) and the already-stalled race-check pattern this invariant reuses verbatim.
- [INV-123](#inv-123-the-completed-session-verdict-none-route-is-bounded--a-no-pr-completed-session-retries-via-dev-new-under-max_retries-branch-c-mirror-no-escalation-ladder-and-a-pr-exists-none-no-qualifying-review-comment-found-still-fails-closed-to-the-inv-12-operator-handoff) and [INV-125](#inv-125-a-resolved-dev-session-id-whose-completion-is_session_completed-cannot-confirm-a-non-terminal-stop-reason-such-as-api_error-a-non-claude-dev-cli-or-an-unreadable-log-with-no-live-wrapper-gets-the-same-bounded-verdict-aware-recovery-as-the-inv-111-self-heal-branch--and-every-marker-present-same-head-fall-through-escalates-to-mark_stalled-never-the-residual-park) ‚Äî two of the five per-entry point-fixes this invariant's class-level backstop complements; INV-125's E2E test replays exactly the park shape INV-125 itself fixed one instance of, to prove the watchdog catches the NEXT instance of that shape class even without a dedicated fix.
- [INV-111](#inv-111-the-dev-wrappers-session-report-is-the-first-durable-write-in-cleanup-gh-resolution-is-re-armed-per-write-against-a-vanished-auth-shim-and-a-same-head-review-fail-with-no-resolvable-session-id-self-heals-via-a-bounded-fresh-dev-new-instead-of-parking-forever) ‚Äî the third of the five point-fixes.
- [INV-09](#inv-09-just_dispatched-skip-rule), [INV-18](#inv-18-cold-start-grace-period-before-stale-detection), [INV-26](#inv-26-stall-decision-excludes-dispatcher-induced-terminations-and-defers-on-live-wrappers) ‚Äî the exemption predicates this invariant reuses rather than reinvents.
- [`dispatcher-flow.md`](dispatcher-flow.md) ¬ß Step 6 ‚Äî the tick-step placement, fingerprint definition, exemptions, and two-tier thresholds.
- `docs/designs/issue-467-liveness-watchdog.md` ‚Äî the full design, including the D3 self-marker-pollution bug caught during implementation and its fix (a separate digest pattern excluding the watchdog's own marker).
