## INV-133: on the codex stdout-classify fallback lane, the severity ratchet MUST NOT demote a `fail` to `pass` unless a WIDER corroboration region (FIRST codex-role turn marker to EOF) agrees no P0/P1-class severity is present ‚Äî a quoted turn-marker line inside the final response can hijack the narrower tail-only scan and must never produce a false PASS

_Triage (issue #236): [machine-checked: tests/unit/test-review-convergence-rules.sh]_

**Rule**: `_review_apply_severity_filter_corroborated` (`lib-review-severity.sh`) ‚Äî not the plain `_review_apply_severity_filter` ‚Äî is the ONLY function that may demote a `fail` verdict to `pass` on the codex stdout-classify fallback lane (`AGENT_VERDICT_SOURCES[i] == "codex-stdout-fallback"`, [INV-132]). It takes:

- `S_tail` ‚Äî the severity of the SAME narrow, LAST-turn-marker-bounded text [INV-132] already scores (`_codex_review_strip_prompt_echo`'s output), extracted via the ordinary `_review_extract_highest_severity` (its per-finding fail-safe is exactly right here ‚Äî the tail IS a findings list).
- `<region-text>` ‚Äî the WIDER region from the **FIRST** codex-role turn marker to EOF ‚Äî every codex-role turn (reasoning, tool-call, AND final-response turns alike), never just the final response (`_codex_review_full_response_region`, `adapters/codex.sh`).

A `fail` whose `S_tail` alone would be demoted at the current round (per `shouldBlockFinding`) is demoted ONLY if `<region-text>` contains NO literal `[P0]`/`[P1]` tag (`_review_region_has_terminal_tag` ‚Äî a bare substring scan, deliberately NOT `_review_extract_highest_severity` on the region; see the dedicated note below on why). If the region DOES contain a `[P0]`/`[P1]` tag, the demotion is REFUSED and the verdict stays `fail` for this round. Every other resolution channel (`artifact`, `comment-fallback`, a codex agent that self-posted through the ordinary poll loop, and every non-codex agent) is UNCHANGED ‚Äî it has no region concept and continues to call the plain `_review_apply_severity_filter`.

**Amendment (review round 1 on this PR itself): the classifier that PRODUCES the verdict must also corroborate, or the filter above never gets a chance to run.** `_review_apply_severity_filter_corroborated` only demotes an EXISTING `fail`; it is a no-op pass-through on any other verdict token. `_codex_review_classify_stdout` (`adapters/codex.sh`) ‚Äî the tail-only-scan classifier that resolves `pass`/`fail`/`malformed` from the raw capture BEFORE the pre-aggregation filter ever runs ‚Äî could itself settle on `pass` directly when the hijack discarded a genuine `[P0]`/`[P1]` finding AND the surviving tail carried no severity tag at all (not merely a masked `[P2]`/`[P3]`, which the pre-amendment classifier still correctly flagged `fail`). In that shape the corroborated filter never even sees the agent, because it was never handed a `fail` to evaluate ‚Äî the exact false PASS this invariant exists to prevent, reachable through a gap one level upstream of the filter this invariant originally scoped. The fix: `_codex_review_classify_stdout` itself now corroborates against `_codex_review_full_response_region` (the identical bare `[P0]`/`[P1]` tag-presence scan, `_review_region_has_terminal_tag`'s own logic inlined) before settling on `pass`, scoped to the SAME genuine-turn-marker condition the tail-only scan already requires (a legacy free-form capture with no turn-marker structure at all has no region concept and keeps its byte-identical whole-capture-scan behavior). A region match at this stage only ever flips `pass`‚Üí`fail`; the round-aware demotion decision remains entirely `_review_apply_severity_filter_corroborated`'s job downstream, unchanged.

**Companion helper ‚Äî `_review_highest_severity_corroborated`**: `AGENT_HIGHEST_SEVERITY[i]` (the array [INV-127]'s `_aggregate_has_p0p1_fail` reads) MUST be populated by this dedicated function on the codex-stdout-fallback lane, never by a bare `_review_extract_highest_severity` call on either text alone (review-round-1 finding on this fix itself, below). It mirrors `_review_apply_severity_filter_corroborated`'s own branch structure: if `S_tail` already blocks at this round, echo `S_tail`; otherwise (a demotion was evaluated), echo the region's own literal tag (`_review_region_terminal_severity`, `P0` > `P1`) when `_review_region_has_terminal_tag` fired ‚Äî the case that MUST report the region's severity, not the tail's, so `_aggregate_has_p0p1_fail` sees the correct terminal-floor evidence for a refused demotion ‚Äî otherwise echo `S_tail` (the demotion was corroborated; `S_tail`'s own P2/P3 is the accurate description of what was actually found).

**Why (the bug this closes, issue #490)**: [INV-132]'s `_codex_review_strip_prompt_echo` locates the final-response boundary by searching for the **LAST** standalone `codex` turn-marker line (column-0, exact word, unfenced, blank-line-preceded ‚Äî the round-3 hardening from the PR that closed #481). Final-response content that QUOTES tool or reviewed-file output can legitimately contain a line of exactly that shape. Such a quoted line wins the LAST-marker search, every finding BEFORE it is discarded, and the surviving tail alone is scored for severity. A genuine `[P1]` can thus be reduced to a `[P2]`-only tail and demoted to a false PASS at round 5+ by [INV-129]'s ratchet.

This is filed as a semantics change, not a fourth heuristic-narrowing round, because three prior rounds (the ones documented in [INV-132]'s own "review round 2/3" fixes) each hardened the marker shape and each produced the adjacent hole: `_codex_review_strip_prompt_echo`'s capture is an unstructured text interleave, so **no textual marker discipline has a floor** ‚Äî any shape requirement (unfenced, column-0, blank-line-preceded) is reproducible by quoted content that merely repeats it. Narrowing the heuristic again would only move the hole, not close it.

**Fix ‚Äî fail-closed corroboration between two independent scans, not another heuristic rung**: `_review_apply_severity_filter_corroborated` requires agreement between `S_tail` (unchanged ‚Äî the existing narrow scan) and `S_region` (new ‚Äî the wide scan from the FIRST marker) before ever trusting a demotion. The wide region is structurally guaranteed to contain any genuine finding on this lane: the region starts immediately after the echoed prompt (the FIRST codex-role turn), and the codex-stdout-fallback lane's raw capture has NOTHING before that point except the echoed `user`-turn prompt ‚Äî never the agent's own findings. So a quoted marker anywhere DEEPER in the transcript can only ever EXCLUDE a real finding from the narrower tail; it can never make a real finding disappear from the wider region too.

```bash
if [[ "${AGENT_VERDICT_SOURCES[$_i]:-}" == "codex-stdout-fallback" && ... ]]; then
  _sev_text=$(_codex_review_strip_prompt_echo "${AGENT_CODEX_LOGS[$_i]}")
  _sev_region_text=$(_codex_review_full_response_region "${AGENT_CODEX_LOGS[$_i]}")
  ...
  AGENT_HIGHEST_SEVERITY[$_i]=$(_review_highest_severity_corroborated "$_sev_text" "$_sev_region_text" "$REVIEW_ROUND")
  AGENT_VERDICTS[$_i]=$(_review_apply_severity_filter_corroborated "${AGENT_VERDICTS[$_i]}" "$_sev_text" "$_sev_region_text" "$REVIEW_ROUND")
else
  AGENT_HIGHEST_SEVERITY[$_i]=$(_review_extract_highest_severity "$_sev_text")
  AGENT_VERDICTS[$_i]=$(_review_apply_severity_filter "${AGENT_VERDICTS[$_i]}" "$_sev_text" "$REVIEW_ROUND")
fi
```

`_codex_review_full_response_region` mirrors `_codex_review_strip_prompt_echo`'s own steps 1-2 (header validation, FIRST `user` marker) verbatim, then diverges at step 3: it stops at the **FIRST** standalone `codex` marker after the `user` marker (same column-0/exact-word/unfenced discipline) rather than tracking the LAST one across the whole file, and returns everything strictly after that point to EOF (minus the same trailing `tokens used: <N>` footer strip).

**Deliberately DROPS the blank-line-precedence requirement** the LAST-marker search uses ‚Äî not a narrowing, an intentional asymmetry (review-round-1 silent-failure-hunter finding on this fix itself). Blank-line-precedence exists in the LAST-marker search to REJECT a spurious inline `codex` word so the search falls back to an EARLIER, genuine marker ‚Äî safe, because "earlier" is exactly the direction a LAST-marker search needs when its top candidate is disqualified. Applying the identical requirement to a FIRST-marker search is backwards: disqualifying the genuinely first marker (e.g. one that directly follows the echoed prompt with no blank separator) would advance the search to a LATER marker instead, excluding a real finding sitting between the two ‚Äî precisely the class of bug this whole invariant exists to close, reintroduced via the borrowed discipline. Accepting the raw FIRST column-0/exact-word/unfenced `codex` line ‚Äî even a spurious one ‚Äî only ever WIDENS the region (it starts no later than the genuine first codex-role turn), which can never exclude a real finding.

**Why the corroboration check is a bare tag scan (`_review_region_has_terminal_tag`), never `_review_extract_highest_severity` on the region (review-round-1 pr-test-analyzer finding)**: `<region-text>` includes reasoning/tool-call turns, which routinely contain ORDINARY numbered prose with no severity concept at all ‚Äî e.g. codex reciting the review checklist verbatim ("1. Design canvas: found in docs/designs/\n2. Worktree: confirmed feat/ branch"). `_review_extract_highest_severity`'s per-finding fail-safe (`[issue #449]`'s own fix for a masked untagged finding) treats ANY numbered line with no `[P0]`-`[P3]` tag as reason to collapse the WHOLE scan to `none` ‚Äî correct for a findings list, but the region is not one. Scoring the region with the full extractor would therefore misclassify a great many ORDINARY clean reviews as carrying P0/P1-class evidence (`none` is itself P0/P1-class in this vocabulary) ‚Äî not the narrow, accepted residual below, but a routine false-fail that permanently blocks convergence on this lane. `_review_region_has_terminal_tag` sidesteps this entirely: it only asks "does a literal `[P0]` or `[P1]` substring appear anywhere in the region," which cannot be tripped by untagged numbered prose.

The same reasoning drove a second, independent bug in the FIRST drafted fix (review-round-1 pr-test-analyzer finding): `AGENT_HIGHEST_SEVERITY[i]` ‚Äî the array [INV-127]'s `_aggregate_has_p0p1_fail` reads to decide whether the round-cap breaker's own terminal-floor gate has evidence to trip on ‚Äî was originally populated from `S_tail` alone, even on a round where the demotion was REFUSED. That reported the tail's masked P2, never the region's real P1, so `_aggregate_has_p0p1_fail` never saw P0/P1-class evidence for exactly the hijack scenario this fix exists for ‚Äî silently defeating the round-cap breaker and turning the documented "eventually stalls to an operator" residual (below) into a genuinely unbounded loop instead. The fix is the dedicated `_review_highest_severity_corroborated` helper (mirrors the filter's own branch structure so the two functions never disagree about which text drove a round's decision): it reports the region's own literal tag (`_review_region_terminal_severity`) specifically for a refused-demotion round, and `S_tail` in every other case.

**Consequence analysis (never a false PASS, sometimes an extra non-converging round)**:
- The hijack shape (a genuine `[P1]` finding, then a quoted blank-line-preceded unfenced `codex` line, then a trailing `[P2]`): `S_tail` = P2 (the `[P1]` is discarded by the quoted marker), region contains the literal `[P1]` tag (still inside the wider region, which starts BEFORE the hijack point) ‚Üí demotion REFUSED ‚Üí stays `fail` at every round, including round 5+, AND `AGENT_HIGHEST_SEVERITY` correctly reports `P1` (not P2) so the round-cap breaker can still trip if the loop repeats. **No false PASS, and the safety-net claim below is actually true.**
- A clean P2-only capture with no hijack (the ordinary [INV-132] reproduction shape): `S_tail` = P2, no `[P0]`/`[P1]` tag anywhere in the region ‚Üí demotion proceeds normally at round 5+ ‚Üí **convergence preserved, no over-correction.**
- A clean review whose reasoning turn recites ordinary numbered prose (e.g. restating the review checklist) with NO severity tags at all: the bare tag scan finds nothing, so the demotion still proceeds normally ‚Äî **no false-fail-forever**, unlike the full-extractor design this fix replaced.
- **Documented residual, accepted**: a reasoning/tool-trace turn that ITSELF quotes a P0/P1 tag (e.g. codex reading a prior review comment via `gh` that itself contained a `[P1]`) also trips the region's tag scan even when the agent's actual final response only carries P2/P3 findings. Demotion is suppressed for that round too ‚Äî a false NON-demotion, not a false PASS. The consequence is "the loop continues an extra round / eventually reaches [INV-127]'s round-cap and stalls to an operator" ‚Äî the SAFE direction this fix optimizes for ‚Äî never a silent false PASS. Chasing this residual with a fifth heuristic rung is explicitly out of scope (issue #490): the class analysis above shows no textual marker discipline has a floor, so the fix changes DEMOTION SEMANTICS (require corroboration) rather than narrowing the marker heuristic yet again.

**Deliberately unchanged**: `_codex_review_strip_prompt_echo` itself (the tail-only scan, [INV-132]) is byte-for-byte unmodified ‚Äî `S_tail` is still exactly what it always was, and `_review_extract_highest_severity` itself (the tail's own extractor) is unmodified. `_review_apply_severity_filter` (the plain, uncorroborated filter) is unmodified and remains the ONLY filter every non-fallback resolution channel uses. `shouldBlockFinding`, `_aggregate_has_p0p1_fail`, and the artifact/comment-poll verdict channels are untouched. No new heuristic rung is added to what counts as a `user`/`codex` turn marker ‚Äî the corroboration region reuses the identical shape discipline, just anchored at the first match instead of the last.

**Status**: **ENFORCED** (closes #490).
**Test**: `tests/unit/test-review-convergence-rules.sh` ‚Äî **TC-CXREGION-001..009** (`_codex_review_full_response_region`, `adapters/codex.sh`: the hijack fixture's genuine `[P1]` and trailing `[P2]` both survive inside the region and the `tokens used:` footer is stripped; the SAME fail-safe contract as `_codex_review_strip_prompt_echo` on a no-header/no-`user`-marker/no-`codex`-marker capture, empty/missing/empty-arg input, indented/trailing-content non-marker lines; a multi-turn capture proves the region INCLUDES earlier reasoning-turn text the tail-only strip helper excludes; the existing over-strip fixture's real finding survives the FIRST-marker search too; **TC-CXREGION-009** [review-round-1 silent-failure-hunter finding] a genuinely first `codex` marker with NO blank line before it is still accepted, not skipped in favor of a later, blank-line-preceded one ‚Äî the region correctly includes a real finding that would otherwise be excluded) and **TC-CORROB-001..014** (`_review_apply_severity_filter_corroborated` / `_review_highest_severity_corroborated`, `lib-review-severity.sh`: the new `codex-review-stdout-turns-blankline-hijack.txt` fixture proves the tail alone scores P2 while the region carries a literal P1 tag; a direct regression pin that the OLD uncorroborated filter DOES demote it at round 5 (proving the bug exists absent the fix); the corroborated filter REFUSES that demotion at round 5 and beyond (round 10 pinned too); the existing clean P2-only fixture still demotes normally under the corroborated filter ‚Äî no over-correction; pass/unavailable/timed-out verdicts pass through unchanged; a tail that already blocks on its own never even reaches the region check; a region with UNTAGGED numbered prose (no literal `[P0]`/`[P1]`) does NOT refuse the demotion ‚Äî the false-fail-forever regression this design closes ‚Äî while a region that DOES carry a literal tag amid the same untagged prose still refuses it; wiring pins for the region-helper call site and the branch-on-resolution-channel routing to the corroborated vs. plain filter; an end-to-end 7-round new-head-every-round simulation confirming the hijack fixture never demotes at ANY round in the ratchet's active range; and **TC-CORROB-011..014** [review-round-1 pr-test-analyzer finding] `_review_highest_severity_corroborated` reports the region's P1 (not the tail's masked P2) for the hijack fixture and confirms that value registers as INV-127 terminal-floor evidence, reports the tail's accurate P2 for a corroborated clean demotion, and ‚Äî the false-fail-forever regression pin ‚Äî still reports the tail's real P2 (not a spurious `none`) for a clean review whose reasoning turn recites untagged numbered prose). Fixture: `tests/unit/fixtures/codex-review-stdout-turns-blankline-hijack.txt` (a genuine `[P1]` finding, then a blank-line-preceded, unfenced, column-0 `codex` line quoted as "adapter output for context", then a trailing `[P2]` ‚Äî the exact hijack shape from the issue). **TC-CXRS-CLS-14/14b/14c** (`tests/unit/test-lib-review-codex.sh`, [review-round-1 finding on this PR itself]: `_codex_review_classify_stdout` on a NEW fixture (`codex-review-stdout-turns-blankline-hijack-notag.txt`) whose tail carries NO tag at all after the hijack (unlike the original fixture, whose tail still retains a masked `[P2]`) ‚Üí `fail`, not `pass` ‚Äî proves the classifier itself now corroborates against the region before the filter ever runs; the original hijack fixture and a genuinely clean turn-marker capture are both pinned unaffected by the amendment).

**Cross-references**:
- [INV-132](#inv-132-the-pre-aggregation-severity-filter-selects-its-scored-text-by-resolution-channel--a-real-branchable-agent_verdict_sources-value-never-agent-identity-or-a-parsed-log-line--and-the-codex-stdout-classify-fallback-route-scores-a-structurally-located-final-response-turn-never-the-raw-echoed-prompt) ‚Äî the tail-only scan (`S_tail`) this invariant corroborates against a second, wider scan before ever trusting a demotion; `_codex_review_strip_prompt_echo` is unmodified.
- [INV-127](#inv-127-a-review-side-divergent-findings-non-convergence-review_convergence_cap-consecutive-failed-substantive-rounds-where-the-severity-ratchet-s-own-p0p1-floor-is-still-failing-is-detected-and-halted--the-breaker-transitions-reviewing--stalled-then-posts-one-structured-reasonreview-round-cap-report-gated-on-an-already-stalled-skip-does-not-gate-on-may_stall_now-mirroring-inv-122s-own-rationale) ‚Äî the safe direction this invariant's residual defers to: a suppressed demotion that never resolves eventually trips the round-cap breaker and stalls to an operator, rather than silently passing.
- [INV-129](#inv-129-review_round-is-a-head-agnostic-series-of-consecutive-decided-failed-substantive-rounds--the-severity-ratchets-blocking-floor-loosens-across-an-active-devreview-loop-new-commit-every-round-not-only-against-a-frozen-head) ‚Äî the ratchet whose demotion decision this invariant gates on the codex stdout-fallback lane specifically.

## INV-134: the DEFAULT `REVIEW_PROTECTED_PATHS` is capability-aware in App mode ‚Äî `.github/workflows/**` is omitted from the built-in default iff `GH_AUTH_MODE=app` AND the minted agent token provably carries the `workflows` scope; every other case (token mode, GitLab, scope absent, malformed conf) keeps the conservative default, and an explicit operator value is NEVER rewritten in either direction

_Triage (issue #236): [machine-checked: tests/unit/test-review-classify.sh]_

**Rule**: [INV-92]'s default `REVIEW_PROTECTED_PATHS` (`.github/workflows/** CODEOWNERS .github/CODEOWNERS`) encodes "the agent's scoped token provably cannot edit `.github/workflows/**`" as a universal premise ‚Äî but that premise is installation-specific. An App-mode installation whose `AGENT_TOKEN_PERMISSIONS` includes `"workflows":"write"` mints an agent token that CAN push workflow-file diffs, yet the stale default still classified every workflow-touching blocking finding `actionable_by_dev_agent=false`, guaranteeing a `dev-actionable=false` escalation ([INV-92] Branch B‚Ä≤) for work the dev agent was fully capable of. `agent_token_has_workflow_scope()` (`lib-review-classify.sh`) ‚Äî a pure `AGENT_TOKEN_PERMISSIONS` config-var read via `jq -e 'has("workflows")'`, NO GitHub API call ‚Äî already existed for exactly this probe but had zero production callers before #488. This invariant wires it into the DEFAULT derivation.

**D1 ‚Äî capability-aware DEFAULT (fail-closed)**: `_review_protected_paths_default_list` (`lib-review-classify.sh`) echoes `CODEOWNERS .github/CODEOWNERS` (workflows omitted) iff `CODE_HOST == "github"` (or unset, which defaults to `"github"` ‚Äî [lib-code-host.sh](../../skills/autonomous-dispatcher/scripts/lib-code-host.sh)'s own `${CODE_HOST:-github}` convention) AND `GH_AUTH_MODE == "app"` AND `agent_token_has_workflow_scope` returns 0; otherwise it echoes the full conservative default (`.github/workflows/** CODEOWNERS .github/CODEOWNERS`). This function is invoked ONLY as the `${VAR-$(...)}` command-substitution default for `REVIEW_PROTECTED_PATHS` ‚Äî bash evaluates a command-substitution default ONLY on the unset branch of a no-colon parameter expansion, so an EXPLICIT value (including `REVIEW_PROTECTED_PATHS=""`) never even calls `_review_protected_paths_default_list`, let alone `agent_token_has_workflow_scope` ‚Äî the capability probe cannot fire, and an operator's explicit list (or explicit empty) is preserved byte-for-byte in every capability outcome, in EITHER direction (it never re-adds workflows protection to an explicit list, and never strips an explicitly-listed workflow pattern).

**Ground truth, and why NOT a GitHub API probe** (the corrected premise from this issue's first drafted revision): the App's *installation grant* (queryable via `gh api /app/installations/<id>`) is NOT the *minted agent token's* permission set ‚Äî `lib-auth.sh` deliberately down-scopes the agent's token to `AGENT_TOKEN_PERMISSIONS` ([INV-79]), so an App WITH `workflows:write` still mints a token WITHOUT it unless the conf explicitly includes it. A token-mint requesting a permission the App grant lacks FAILS outright, so a RUNNING wrapper with `"workflows"` present in `AGENT_TOKEN_PERMISSIONS` is itself proof both of conf intent AND of the underlying grant ‚Äî `agent_token_has_workflow_scope`'s config-var read is authoritative for App mode; an API probe would answer the wrong question (the grant, not the mint) and is explicitly out of scope.

**Mode gate ‚Äî why token/PAT mode and GitLab keep the conservative default even with `"workflows":"write"` present in the var**: `AGENT_TOKEN_PERMISSIONS` only drives App-mode token MINTING ([INV-79]); in `GH_AUTH_MODE=token` the agent inherits the wrapper's PAT verbatim, so the var's contents say nothing about the PAT's actual scopes ‚Äî reading it as a capability signal outside App mode would be a fabricated (optimistic) claim. GitLab has no GitHub-App equivalent at all (¬ß5.1 of `provider-spec.md`). Both installs keep the pre-#488 explicit-override workaround (`REVIEW_PROTECTED_PATHS="CODEOWNERS .github/CODEOWNERS"` in `autonomous.conf`) as the sanctioned unlock mechanism ‚Äî this invariant does not add capability detection for either.

**Host gate (added post-merge-review, PR #498 round 1 [P2] finding #1) ‚Äî why the check is ALSO gated on `CODE_HOST == "github"`**: `GH_AUTH_MODE`/`AGENT_TOKEN_PERMISSIONS` are read by `lib-auth.sh`'s GitHub-App token-minting path ONLY. On a pure GitLab run (`CODE_HOST=gitlab`) that retains a leftover or copy-pasted `GH_AUTH_MODE=app` + `"workflows":"write"` in `AGENT_TOKEN_PERMISSIONS` ‚Äî e.g. an operator migrating a project's conf from GitHub to GitLab without pruning the now-inert GitHub-App fragment ‚Äî no scoped GitHub App token is minted at all, so those two vars prove nothing about what the review agent's actual GitLab credential can push. Without the host gate, `_review_protected_paths_default_list` would still omit `.github/workflows/**` from the default in that configuration purely because the leftover vars happen to be set, which breaks the documented GitLab fail-closed guarantee (the "Mode gate" paragraph above). The gate mirrors `lib-code-host.sh`'s own `${CODE_HOST:-github}` convention so an unset `CODE_HOST` (the common single-host-GitHub installation) behaves identically to `CODE_HOST=github` ‚Äî no regression for the common case.

**D2 ‚Äî prompt/derivation single source of truth**: `review_protected_paths_prompt_rule` (the [INV-92] `review_protected_paths_prompt_rule`/[#301] one-source-of-truth function) reads the SAME `$REVIEW_PROTECTED_PATHS` D1 populates, so its advertised protected-glob list already reflects the capability-aware default with zero additional wiring. Additionally, the per-finding `requires_privileged_token` guidance ‚Äî previously a hardcoded "(it does by default)" claim about the token's workflow scope ‚Äî now resolves the SAME `CODE_HOST`-gated `agent_token_has_workflow_scope`/`GH_AUTH_MODE` check at prompt-render time and states the ACTUAL scope value for the running configuration, so the prompt can never assert a scope gap the running deployment disproves (and, on a GitLab run with a leftover App-mode conf fragment, correctly states `false` rather than a host-gate-violating `true`).

**D3 ‚Äî anti-forge preservation ([INV-92] invariant, unchanged in shape)**: the wrapper's authoritative override (`review_classify_artifact_dev_actionable`) is untouched ‚Äî every pattern REMAINING in the effective `REVIEW_PROTECTED_PATHS` still forces effective `actionable_by_dev_agent=false` regardless of what the agent asserts, and an agent-authored `false` is never promoted to `true`. The ONLY thing D1 changes is which patterns are IN the default set to begin with; once a path is (or isn't) in the effective set, the override behaves byte-identically to pre-#488. A `.github/workflows/ci.yml` finding under a capability-unlocked config is simply never protected in the first place ‚Äî there is no forge to guard against for a path the effective list doesn't cover, and CODEOWNERS remains protected in EVERY capability outcome (D1 never touches it).

**D4 ‚Äî classification-time stall diagnostics**: the dispatcher's `reason=non_actionable_finding` stall comment (both [INV-92] Branch B‚Ä≤ in `handle_completed_session_routing` and the same-HEAD `_same_head_verdict_aware_recovery` dev-actionable=false branch) previously could not name WHICH `REVIEW_PROTECTED_PATHS` pattern forced the classification ‚Äî the dispatcher only ever sees the trailer's coarse `dev-actionable=false` bit, while the per-finding path lives only in the review wrapper at classification time. Fix: `review_classify_artifact_matched_patterns` (`lib-review-classify.sh`) echoes the sorted/unique protected-path pattern(s) matched by an artifact's blocking findings (fail-empty on non-JSON/no-jq, mirroring `review_classify_artifact_dev_actionable`'s own fail-open posture). `autonomous-review.sh` stashes each FAILing agent's matched patterns into a surviving `AGENT_MATCHED_PATTERNS[]` global (sibling of `AGENT_DEV_ACTIONABLE[]`, computed from the SAME live `_art_json` for the SAME TOCTOU-safety reason ‚Äî the per-run artifact dir is `rm -rf`'d before the emit site runs). `lib-dispatch.sh::_inv92_matched_patterns` reads the newest such marker off the issue; both dispatcher stall-notice call sites surface the pattern list + conf-lever pointer when the marker is present, and fall back to the pre-#488 generic wording (unchanged, no new failure mode) when it is absent.

**D4 aggregation is INDEPENDENT of the aggregate `dev-actionable` bit (fixed post-merge-review, PR #498 round 1 [P2] finding #2)**: `_AGG_MATCHED_PATTERNS` is computed by scanning every FAILing agent's `AGENT_MATCHED_PATTERNS[]` UNCONDITIONALLY ‚Äî never gated on `_AGG_DEV_ACTIONABLE == "false"`. The first cut of this feature gated the collection loop on the aggregate, which silently dropped the diagnostic for a MIXED FAIL: one blocking finding forced non-actionable by a protected-path match, plus a second, ordinary finding that IS actionable. The aggregate is an OR ("true" iff ANY blocking finding is actionable), so a mixed FAIL has `_AGG_DEV_ACTIONABLE=true` even though D4's own contract ‚Äî "whenever the derivation forces ‚â•1 blocking finding non-actionable" ‚Äî still requires naming the matched pattern for the one finding that WAS forced. The findings comment is now posted whenever `_AGG_MATCHED_PATTERNS` is non-empty, REGARDLESS of the aggregate outcome; its lead sentence branches on `_AGG_DEV_ACTIONABLE` so the wording never overclaims total non-actionability in the mixed case ("This substantive FAIL was classified not dev-agent-actionable‚Ä¶" only when aggregate is `false`; a distinct "includes at least one blocking finding matched‚Ä¶the dev agent will still be re-dispatched for the remaining actionable finding(s)" sentence when aggregate is `true`). The dispatcher-side consumers ([INV-92] Branch B‚Ä≤ and `_same_head_verdict_aware_recovery`) are unaffected by this: both are reached ONLY when the trailer's `dev-actionable=false`, so the marker now also being posted in the aggregate=true (mixed) case is inert for those call sites ‚Äî it is simply available on the issue for a human skimming the mixed-FAIL comment thread.

**D4 fail-safety (`|| true` on the `_inv92_matched_patterns` pipe, load-bearing at the direct call site)**: `lib-dispatch.sh` runs under `set -euo pipefail`. Without a trailing `|| true`, a transient `itp_list_comments` failure (rate-limit/auth/network blip ‚Äî exactly the class this function's contract names) inside `_inv92_matched_patterns` propagates a non-zero pipe exit status through `pipefail`, and the caller's plain `_na_patterns="$(_inv92_matched_patterns "$issue_num")"` assignment then aborts the ENTIRE function under `errexit` ‚Äî `mark_stalled` never runs. `handle_completed_session_routing`'s Branch B‚Ä≤ is reached via a bare top-level statement at its `dispatcher-tick.sh:693` call site (no enclosing `if`/`&&`/`||` to suppress errexit there), so this is directly exploitable: one transient API hiccup while rendering a COSMETIC diagnostic sentence could abort the whole dispatcher tick, leaving every other in-flight issue that tick un-scanned. The sibling call inside `_same_head_verdict_aware_recovery` is reached only via `&& return 0` (itself inside an outer `if handle_pending_dev_pr_exists ...; then`), where bash's errexit-suppression-for-if-conditions rule shields the whole call chain regardless ‚Äî but the `|| true` is added there too as defense-in-depth (matching this codebase's own [INV-108] precedent of never relying on ambient `set -e` in code reachable via an `if` condition).

**Producer**: `lib-review-classify.sh` (`_review_protected_paths_default_list`, `review_classify_artifact_matched_patterns`, the updated `review_protected_paths_prompt_rule`) + `autonomous-review.sh` (`AGENT_MATCHED_PATTERNS[]` population, the D4 findings-comment post). **Consumer**: `lib-dispatch.sh::_inv92_matched_patterns` + both [INV-92] stall-notice call sites.

**Status**: **ENFORCED** as of #488. Delta on top of [INV-92](#inv-92-a-review-blocking-finding-the-dev-agent-provably-cannot-act-on-protected-path--missing-token-scope-is-not-routed-to-dev-resume--the-wrapper-classifies-each-findings-actionability-and-the-dispatcher-escalates-a-non-actionable-verdict-to-stalled) and [INV-79] (the two-token split / scoped agent credential). No state-machine label edges change (same `mark_stalled` call sites [INV-92] already reuses).

**Tests**: `tests/unit/test-review-classify.sh` ‚Äî the D1 capability-aware-default matrix (App+scope‚Üíomits workflows; App+scope-absent/empty/malformed/no-jq/unset‚Üíconservative; token-mode+scope-present-in-var‚Üíconservative, the mode gate; `GH_AUTH_MODE` unset‚Üíconservative; `TC-INV134-D1-14`: `CODE_HOST=gitlab` + leftover App-mode conf‚Üíconservative, the host gate; `TC-INV134-D1-15`/`-16`: `CODE_HOST` explicitly `"github"` and unset both still omit workflows under a capability-unlocked config, no regression from the host gate), explicit-value-never-rewritten (empty and non-empty, under both capability outcomes, including an explicit list that itself names `.github/workflows/**`), the capability-probe short-circuit on an explicit value, the D2 prompt-rule wiring (glob list + `requires_privileged_token` note track the same capability check including the host gate ‚Äî `TC-INV134-D2-06`/`-06b` pin the `CODE_HOST=gitlab` case ‚Äî and the retired "it does by default" literal is gone), the D3 anti-forge regression under both capability outcomes, and `review_classify_artifact_matched_patterns` (multi-finding sorted-unique, no-match empty, non-JSON fail-empty, duplicate dedup). `tests/unit/test-autonomous-review-inv134-matched-patterns.sh` (wrapper-side D4 block, `awk`-extracted per the `test-autonomous-review-diffcap-wiring.sh` precedent: comment posted + marker + conf-lever pointer whenever a pattern was recorded, REGARDLESS of the aggregate; `TC-INV134-D4-06` no comment when NO pattern was recorded even with aggregate=true; `TC-INV134-D4-07` no comment on an unrecorded non-actionable cause; `TC-INV134-D4-14` the MIXED-failure regression ‚Äî aggregate=true WITH a recorded pattern still posts the comment, with wording that does not overclaim total non-actionability; only FAILing agents contribute). `tests/unit/test-handle-completed-session-routing.sh` / `tests/unit/test-issue-466-crashed-session-recovery.sh` (dispatcher-side: the stall notice surfaces the marker's pattern list + conf-lever pointer when present, and is byte-identical to the pre-#488 generic wording when absent; `_inv92_matched_patterns` directly, fail-empty on transport failure; and a `set -euo pipefail` fresh-subshell regression pin, mirroring `test-liveness-watchdog.sh`'s TC-LIVENESS-075..078 precedent, proving `mark_stalled` is still reached ‚Äî not aborted ‚Äî when the internal `itp_list_comments` call transiently fails at both call sites).

**Cross-references**:
- [INV-92](#inv-92-a-review-blocking-finding-the-dev-agent-provably-cannot-act-on-protected-path--missing-token-scope-is-not-routed-to-dev-resume--the-wrapper-classifies-each-findings-actionability-and-the-dispatcher-escalates-a-non-actionable-verdict-to-stalled) ‚Äî the classification + escalation invariant this default feeds; D3 preserves its anti-forge override unchanged.
- [`review-agent-flow.md`](review-agent-flow.md) ‚Äî the per-finding classification step, updated with the capability-aware default + D4 marker.
